---
title: "ABG-VBG Analysis"
author: "Brian Locke, Anila Mehta"
editor: visual
params:
  run_mode: "full" # options: "pilot" or "full"
  pilot_frac: 1 # used only when run_mode == "pilot"
  sample_seed: 20251206
  data_dir: "data"
  results_dir: "Results"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    pdf-engine: lualatex
    keep-tex: true
    hyperrefoptions:
      - draft
    pdf-engine-opts:
      - --cnf-line=dest_names_size=300000
    mainfont: "Latin Modern Roman"
    code-block-bg: false
    geometry:
      - landscape
      - margin=0.8in
    fig-width: 11
    fig-height: 7
    df-print: kable
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{float}
  - \usepackage{placeins}
  - \usepackage{array}
  - \graphicspath{{../}{./}}
fontsize: 11pt
execute:
  echo: true
  message: false
  warning: false
---

```{r todo-notes}
#| include: false
#| results: "hide"
INTERNAL_TODO_LINES <- c(
  "# Internal Future-Work Notes",
  "",
  "- ABG residual imbalance >0.10: consider redefining target population, stratified standardization, or overlap restriction.",
  "- Separation/nonconvergence in adjusted outcome models: consider penalized logistic models, level collapsing, and a formal reporting policy.",
  "- Possible discontinuity at ABG 45 mmHg CO2 and VBG 50 mmHg CO2: start with raw binning and LOESS, then consider regression-discontinuity methods only if supported.",
  "- GBM stopping rule: consider stop.method = 'es.mean' only if smd.max remains unstable on a future full run."
)
```

# Data Pre-Processing and Unweighted Analyses

This code pulls in the master database (a STATA file) and does some initial cleaning - this will only need to be run once, and then the data can be accessed in the usual way.

```{r setup-table-helpers}
#| code-block-title: "Table helper functions"
#| include: false
# setup-packages chunk is the authoritative loader.
stopifnot(
  requireNamespace("kableExtra", quietly = TRUE),
  requireNamespace("gtsummary", quietly = TRUE),
  requireNamespace("purrr", quietly = TRUE)
)

# globally tighten gtsummary/gt tables (smaller font + tighter padding)
gtsummary::theme_gtsummary_compact()

project_root <- here::here()
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = project_root)

# keep figures anchored in PDF to reduce heading-only/blank pages
if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
  knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.pos = "H")
}

# helper: scale generic data.frames for PDF output
kable_pdf <- function(df, caption = NULL, font_size = 7) {
  kbl <- knitr::kable(df, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, caption = caption)
  kbl <- kableExtra::kable_styling(
    kbl,
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"),
    font_size     = font_size,
    full_width    = TRUE,
    position      = "center"
  )
  mark_float_emitted()
  kbl
}

# helper: resolve a notebook display path relative to the repo root so the
# canonical render path never depends on the notebook subdirectory.
display_path <- function(path, must_exist = TRUE) {
  vapply(path, function(one_path) {
    path_abs <- tryCatch(
      fs::path_abs(one_path, start = project_root),
      error = function(e) NA_character_
    )
    if (is.na(path_abs)) {
      stop("Could not resolve display path: ", one_path)
    }
    if (must_exist && !file.exists(path_abs)) {
      stop("Missing display artifact: ", path_abs)
    }
    if (!(fs::path_has_parent(path_abs, project_root) || identical(path_abs, project_root))) {
      stop("Display path escapes project root: ", path_abs)
    }
    fs::path_rel(path_abs, start = project_root)
  }, character(1), USE.NAMES = FALSE)
}

# helper: pretty, relative paths for PDF output
pretty_path <- function(p) {
  vapply(p, function(one_path) {
    out <- tryCatch(display_path(one_path, must_exist = FALSE)[[1]], error = function(e) NA_character_)
    if (is.na(out) || out == "." || grepl("^\\.\\.", out)) {
      return(basename(one_path))
    }
    out
  }, character(1), USE.NAMES = FALSE)
}

# helper: emit inline notebook graphics through one canonical path. For PDF
# renders we cap the height explicitly so headings plus image can fit on the
# page without triggering TeX pagination loops.
render_inline_graphic <- function(file,
                                  width = "0.94\\linewidth",
                                  height = "0.72\\textheight") {
  inline_path <- display_path(file)
  if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
    out <- c(
      "\\begin{center}",
      sprintf(
        "\\includegraphics[width=%s,height=%s,keepaspectratio]{%s}",
        width, height, inline_path
      ),
      "\\end{center}"
    )
    return(knitr::asis_output(paste(out, collapse = "\n")))
  }
  knitr::include_graphics(inline_path)
}

# Canonical PDF path: export all figure/table artifacts and reserve rendered
# output for manuscript-facing displays plus compact provenance/status summaries.
# QA/debug figures and source-heavy diagnostics remain sidecar artifacts.
VALIDATION_INLINE_ANALYSIS_PREVIEWS <- FALSE

# helper: normalize manuscript-facing text to a plain-ASCII representation so
# PDF labels/captions do not depend on platform-specific glyph handling.
safe_manuscript_text <- function(x) {
  if (is.null(x) || !length(x)) return(x)
  x <- as.character(x)
  replacements <- c(
    "\u00a0" = " ",
    "\u2010" = "-",
    "\u2011" = "-",
    "\u2012" = "-",
    "\u2013" = "-",
    "\u2014" = "-",
    "\u2212" = "-",
    "\u2026" = "...",
    "\u03bc" = "u",
    "\u00b5" = "u",
    "\u2264" = "<=",
    "\u2265" = ">=",
    "\u0394" = "Delta",
    "\u03b4" = "delta",
    "\u03b2" = "beta",
    "\u2082" = "2",
    "\u00b2" = "^2",
    "\u00b3" = "^3",
    "\u2080" = "0",
    "\u2081" = "1",
    "\u2083" = "3",
    "\u2084" = "4",
    "\u2085" = "5",
    "\u2086" = "6",
    "\u2087" = "7",
    "\u2088" = "8",
    "\u2089" = "9"
  )
  for (i in seq_along(replacements)) {
    x <- gsub(names(replacements)[i], replacements[[i]], x, fixed = TRUE)
  }
  x <- iconv(x, from = "", to = "ASCII//TRANSLIT", sub = "?")
  x <- gsub("\\s+", " ", x)
  trimws(x)
}

# helper: flag non-ASCII manuscript labels early so validation renders fail
# before the final PDF silently substitutes or drops glyphs.
is_ascii_text <- function(x) {
  if (is.null(x) || !length(x)) return(logical(0))
  !grepl("[^ -~]", x)
}

# helper: derive one stable manuscript caption from the centralized registry.
manuscript_caption <- function(label, title) {
  label <- safe_manuscript_text(label)
  title <- safe_manuscript_text(title)
  stopifnot(length(label) == length(title))
  ifelse(!nzchar(title), label, paste0(label, ". ", title))
}

# helper: collapse component-level validation states into one build status.
collapse_validation_status <- function(status_values) {
  status_values <- unique(status_values[!is.na(status_values) & nzchar(status_values)])
  if ("FAIL" %in% status_values) return("FAIL")
  if ("PASS_WITH_WARNINGS" %in% status_values) return("PASS_WITH_WARNINGS")
  "PASS"
}

# helper: strip manual "Table X." prefixes in PDF to avoid double numbering
strip_manual_table_number <- function(caption) {
  if (is.null(caption)) return(caption)
  if (!knitr::is_latex_output()) return(caption)
  cap <- gsub("^\\s*(\\*\\*)?Table\\s+[0-9A-Za-z]+\\.?\\s*:?\\s*", "", caption)
  cap
}

# helper: escape LaTeX special chars in plain captions
escape_latex_text <- function(x, force = knitr::is_latex_output()) {
  if (is.null(x)) return(x)
  if (!isTRUE(force)) return(x)
  gsub("([\\\\%$&#{}_\\^~])", "\\\\\\1", x, perl = TRUE)
}

# helper: strip markdown/LaTeX numbering and formatting so captions can be
# safely reused in both inline Quarto output and standalone PDF exports.
plain_caption_text <- function(caption) {
  if (is.null(caption) || !nzchar(caption)) return("")
  cap <- strip_manual_table_number(caption)
  cap <- gsub("\\*\\*", "", cap)
  cap <- gsub("\\s+", " ", cap)
  trimws(cap)
}

# helper: replace CO2 labels with LaTeX-safe subscripts in table text so PDF
# tables render predictably even when the visible code uses plain ASCII labels.
latex_safe_table_text <- function(x, force = knitr::is_latex_output()) {
  if (!isTRUE(force) || is.null(x) || !length(x)) return(x)
  out <- x
  out <- gsub("PaCO₂", "PaCO\\\\textsubscript{2}", out, fixed = TRUE)
  out <- gsub("PvCO₂", "PvCO\\\\textsubscript{2}", out, fixed = TRUE)
  out <- gsub("PaCO2", "PaCO\\\\textsubscript{2}", out, fixed = TRUE)
  out <- gsub("PvCO2", "PvCO\\\\textsubscript{2}", out, fixed = TRUE)
  out
}

# helper: build one plain-text heading string for manually managed table
# displays, where the notebook wants explicit manuscript labels.
table_heading_block <- function(title = NULL, display_label = NULL) {
  title <- plain_caption_text(title)
  label <- if (is.null(display_label) || !nzchar(display_label)) "" else trimws(as.character(display_label))
  heading <- trimws(paste(if (nzchar(label)) paste0(label, ".") else "", title))
  if (!nzchar(heading)) return("")
  if (!knitr::is_latex_output()) return(paste0("**", heading, "**\n\n"))
  heading <- latex_safe_table_text(escape_latex_text(heading))
  paste0("\\begin{center}\\textbf{", heading, "}\\end{center}")
}

table_note_block <- function(note = NULL) {
  if (is.null(note) || length(note) == 0L) return(character())
  note <- paste(safe_manuscript_text(note), collapse = " ")
  if (!nzchar(note)) return(character())
  if (!knitr::is_latex_output()) return(paste0("*Note.* ", note))
  paste0(
    "\\par\\medskip\\noindent\\footnotesize\\textit{Note.} ",
    latex_safe_table_text(escape_latex_text(note)),
    "\\normalsize\\par"
  )
}

# helper: emit a table heading plus one or more LaTeX/Markdown table blocks,
# keeping page-break and landscape behavior explicit in one place.
render_table_blocks <- function(table_blocks, title = NULL, display_label = NULL,
                                landscape = FALSE, page_break_before = FALSE) {
  heading <- table_heading_block(title = title, display_label = display_label)
  out <- c(if (nzchar(heading)) heading else character(0), table_blocks)
  if (knitr::is_latex_output() && isTRUE(landscape)) {
    out <- c("\\begin{landscape}", out, "\\end{landscape}")
  }
  if (knitr::is_latex_output() && isTRUE(page_break_before)) {
    out <- c("\\clearpage", out)
  }
  mark_float_emitted()
  knitr::asis_output(paste(out, collapse = "\n\n"))
}

# helper: mark points where a visible table/figure has been emitted; this is a
# semantic hook more than a data object, but it keeps display code readable.
mark_float_emitted <- function() {
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: clean manuscript/supplement table CSVs for publication-facing export.
# Run provenance remains in diagnostic/provenance artifacts written elsewhere.
publication_table_csv_df <- function(df) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df))
  out <- as.data.frame(df)
  if ("panel" %in% names(out)) {
    if ("Table section" %in% names(out)) {
      out <- dplyr::select(out, -dplyr::any_of("panel"))
    } else {
      names(out)[names(out) == "panel"] <- "Table section"
    }
  }
  dplyr::select(out, -dplyr::any_of(c("run_id", "run_ts")))
}

write_publication_table_csv <- function(df, path, row_names = FALSE) {
  write_csv_safely(
    publication_table_csv_df(df),
    path,
    row_names = row_names,
    include_run_metadata = FALSE
  )
}

publication_table_csv_stubs <- c(
  "table1_combined",
  "table_1_baseline_characteristics_analytic_cohort",
  "table_2_weighted_categorical_outcomes",
  "table_s1_inclusion_criteria",
  "table_s2_crude_threelevel",
  "table_s3_gbm_threelevel",
  "table_s4_missingness_primary_analysis",
  "table_s5_mi_diagnostic_summary",
  "table_s6_stacked_ror_cat3",
  "table_s7_stacked_ror_spline_readouts_5mm",
  "table_s8_offset_scan_summary",
  "table_s9_category_risk_differences",
  "table_s10_spline_risk_difference_readouts_5mm",
  "table_s11_offset_scan_risk_difference",
  "table_s12_baseline_outcome_rates_and_odds",
  "table_s13_categorical_likelihood_ratios",
  "table_s14_spline_likelihood_ratios_5mm",
  "table_s15_offset_scan_likelihood_ratio"
)

write_table_csv_for_stub <- function(df, path, file_stub, row_names = FALSE) {
  if (file_stub %in% publication_table_csv_stubs) {
    return(write_publication_table_csv(df, path, row_names = row_names))
  }
  write_csv_safely(df, path, row_names = row_names)
}

# helper: flush accumulated LaTeX floats at important manuscript boundaries so
# tables and figures stay near the code that generated them.
float_barrier <- function(mode = c("soft", "section", "hard", "flush")) {
  if (!knitr::is_latex_output()) return(invisible(NULL))
  mode <- match.arg(mode)
  cmd <- switch(
    mode,
    soft = "\\FloatBarrier\\nopagebreak",
    section = "\\FloatBarrier\\nopagebreak\\par\\noindent",
    hard = "\\FloatBarrier\\clearpage",
    flush = "\\FloatBarrier\\pagebreak"
  )
  knitr::asis_output(cmd)
}

# helper: render full tables for PDF (no previews)
render_table_pdf <- function(df, caption, file_stub,
                             wide = FALSE,
                             digits = 2,
                             max_cols = 6,
                             landscape = NULL,
                             display_label = NULL,
                             caption_mode = c("plain", "numbered", "none"),
                             page_break_before = FALSE,
                             note = NULL) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df))
  caption_mode <- match.arg(caption_mode)
  out_csv <- results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".csv"))
  write_table_csv_for_stub(df, out_csv, file_stub, row_names = FALSE)

  if (is.null(landscape)) landscape <- (wide || ncol(df) > max_cols)
  n_cols <- ncol(df)
  parts <- if (n_cols <= max_cols) list(df) else {
    idx <- split(seq_len(n_cols), ceiling(seq_len(n_cols) / max_cols))
    lapply(idx, function(ii) df[, ii, drop = FALSE])
  }
  use_longtable <- nrow(df) > 40 && length(parts) == 1L && !isTRUE(landscape)

  build_kbl <- function(df_part, cap) {
    cap <- plain_caption_text(cap)
    cap_numbered <- if (identical(caption_mode, "numbered")) {
      escape_latex_text(latex_safe_table_text(cap))
    } else {
      NULL
    }
    kbl <- knitr::kable(
      df_part,
      format    = "latex",
      booktabs  = TRUE,
      longtable = use_longtable,
      caption   = cap_numbered,
      digits    = digits
    )
    kbl <- kableExtra::kable_styling(
      kbl,
      latex_options = if (use_longtable) c("repeat_header") else c("hold_position", "scale_down"),
      font_size     = if (ncol(df_part) > max_cols || wide) 6 else 7,
      full_width    = FALSE,
      position      = "center"
    )
    latex_safe_table_text(as.character(kbl))
  }

  letters_part <- LETTERS[seq_along(parts)]
  out <- unlist(lapply(seq_along(parts), function(i) {
    cap_i <- if (length(parts) == 1L) caption else paste0(caption, " (Part ", letters_part[i], ")")
    build_kbl(parts[[i]], cap_i)
  }))
  out <- c(out, table_note_block(note))
  if (identical(caption_mode, "numbered")) {
    mark_float_emitted()
    return(knitr::asis_output(paste(out, collapse = "\n\n")))
  }
  title_i <- if (identical(caption_mode, "none")) NULL else caption
  render_table_blocks(
    out,
    title = title_i,
    display_label = display_label,
    landscape = landscape,
    page_break_before = page_break_before
  )
}

# helper: conditionally show a rendered table in the notebook while still
# always exporting the CSV/PDF artifact needed downstream.
render_table_pdf_maybe <- function(df, caption, file_stub,
                                   wide = FALSE,
                                   digits = 2,
                                   max_cols = 6,
                                   landscape = NULL,
                                   show = TRUE,
                                   display_label = NULL,
                                   caption_mode = c("plain", "numbered", "none"),
                                   note = NULL) {
  caption_mode <- match.arg(caption_mode)
  if (!isTRUE(show)) {
    write_table_csv_for_stub(df, results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".csv")), file_stub, row_names = FALSE)
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  tbl <- render_table_pdf(
    df,
    caption = caption,
    file_stub = file_stub,
    wide = wide,
    digits = digits,
    max_cols = max_cols,
    landscape = landscape,
    display_label = display_label,
    caption_mode = caption_mode,
    note = note
  )
  if (isTRUE(show)) {
    return(tbl)
  }
  invisible(NULL)
}

# helper: render one numbered manuscript table as multiple explicit panels.
# This avoids automatic wide-table splitting, which can detach the final column
# from the table body in LaTeX output.
render_panel_table_pdf <- function(panels, caption, file_stub,
                                   digits = 2,
                                   display_label = NULL,
                                   landscape = TRUE,
                                   page_break_before = TRUE,
                                   csv_df = NULL,
                                   panel_page_breaks = FALSE,
                                   scale_down = TRUE,
                                   font_size = 7,
                                   longtable = FALSE,
                                   column_widths = NULL,
                                   note = NULL) {
  stopifnot(is.list(panels), length(panels) > 0L)
  panel_names <- names(panels)
  if (is.null(panel_names) || any(!nzchar(panel_names))) {
    panel_names <- paste("Panel", LETTERS[seq_along(panels)])
  }
  stopifnot(all(vapply(panels, is.data.frame, logical(1L))))

  if (is.null(csv_df)) {
    csv_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
      lapply(seq_along(panels), function(idx) {
        dplyr::mutate(panels[[idx]], panel = panel_names[[idx]], .before = 1L)
      })
    )
  }
  write_table_csv_for_stub(csv_df, results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".csv")), file_stub, row_names = FALSE)

  build_kbl <- function(df_part) {
    kbl <- knitr::kable(
      df_part,
      format = "latex",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      longtable = longtable,
      digits = digits
    )
    latex_options <- if (isTRUE(longtable)) {
      c("repeat_header")
    } else {
      c("hold_position", if (isTRUE(scale_down)) "scale_down")
    }
    kbl <- kableExtra::kable_styling(
      kbl,
      latex_options = latex_options,
      font_size = font_size,
      full_width = FALSE,
      position = "center"
    )
    if (!is.null(column_widths)) {
      for (col_name in intersect(names(column_widths), names(df_part))) {
        kbl <- kableExtra::column_spec(
          kbl,
          which(names(df_part) == col_name),
          width = column_widths[[col_name]]
        )
      }
    }
    latex_safe_table_text(as.character(kbl))
  }

  panel_blocks <- unlist(lapply(seq_along(panels), function(idx) {
    panel_title <- paste0("Panel ", LETTERS[[idx]], ". ", panel_names[[idx]])
    break_cmd <- if (knitr::is_latex_output() && isTRUE(panel_page_breaks) && idx > 1L) "\\clearpage" else character(0)
    if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
      c(
        break_cmd,
        paste0("\\medskip\\noindent\\textbf{", escape_latex_text(latex_safe_table_text(panel_title)), "}\\par"),
        build_kbl(panels[[idx]])
      )
    } else {
      c(paste0("**", panel_title, "**"), as.character(knitr::kable(panels[[idx]], digits = digits)))
    }
  }))
  panel_blocks <- c(panel_blocks, table_note_block(note))

  render_table_blocks(
    panel_blocks,
    title = caption,
    display_label = display_label,
    landscape = landscape,
    page_break_before = page_break_before
  )
}

definition_table_value <- function(x, latex = knitr::is_latex_output()) {
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0L || all(is.na(x))) return("")
  value <- paste(safe_manuscript_text(x), collapse = " ")
  if (!isTRUE(latex)) return(value)
  value <- latex_safe_table_text(escape_latex_text(value, force = TRUE), force = TRUE)
  value <- gsub("; ", ";\\allowbreak{} ", value, fixed = TRUE)
  value <- gsub("\\_", "\\_\\allowbreak{}", value, fixed = TRUE)
  value
}

definition_table_blocks <- function(panels,
                                    panel_page_breaks = TRUE,
                                    latex = knitr::is_latex_output(),
                                    field_order = c(
                                      "Criterion / variable",
                                      "Code/source field",
                                      "Role in analysis",
                                      "Note"
                                    )) {
  stopifnot(is.list(panels), length(panels) > 0L)
  panel_names <- names(panels)
  if (is.null(panel_names) || any(!nzchar(panel_names))) {
    panel_names <- paste("Panel", LETTERS[seq_along(panels)])
  }
  field_labels <- c(
    "Criterion / variable" = "Criterion / variable",
    "Code/source field" = "Code/source field",
    "Role in analysis" = "Role in analysis",
    "Note" = "Notes"
  )

  unlist(lapply(seq_along(panels), function(idx) {
    panel_df <- panels[[idx]]
    stopifnot(is.data.frame(panel_df))
    active_fields <- intersect(field_order, names(panel_df))
    panel_title <- paste0("Panel ", LETTERS[[idx]], ". ", panel_names[[idx]])
    if (isTRUE(latex)) {
      break_cmd <- if (isTRUE(panel_page_breaks) && idx > 1L) "\\clearpage" else character(0)
      panel_header <- paste0(
        "\\medskip\\noindent\\textbf{",
        escape_latex_text(latex_safe_table_text(panel_title, force = TRUE), force = TRUE),
        "}\\par\\medskip"
      )
      item_blocks <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(panel_df)), function(row_idx) {
        field_lines <- unlist(lapply(active_fields, function(field) {
          label <- escape_latex_text(field_labels[[field]], force = TRUE)
          value <- definition_table_value(panel_df[[field]][[row_idx]], latex = TRUE)
          paste0("\\textbf{", label, ":} ", value, "\\par")
        }))
        c(
          "\\noindent\\begin{minipage}{\\linewidth}\\small\\raggedright",
          field_lines,
          "\\end{minipage}\\par\\vspace{0.55em}"
        )
      }))
      c(break_cmd, panel_header, item_blocks)
    } else {
      item_blocks <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(panel_df)), function(row_idx) {
        field_lines <- unlist(lapply(active_fields, function(field) {
          label <- field_labels[[field]]
          value <- definition_table_value(panel_df[[field]][[row_idx]], latex = FALSE)
          paste0("**", label, ":** ", value)
        }))
        c(field_lines, "")
      }))
      c(paste0("**", panel_title, "**"), item_blocks)
    }
  }), use.names = FALSE)
}

render_definition_table_pdf <- function(panels, caption, file_stub,
                                        display_label = NULL,
                                        csv_df = NULL,
                                        page_break_before = TRUE,
                                        panel_page_breaks = TRUE) {
  stopifnot(is.list(panels), length(panels) > 0L)
  panel_names <- names(panels)
  if (is.null(panel_names) || any(!nzchar(panel_names))) {
    panel_names <- paste("Panel", LETTERS[seq_along(panels)])
  }
  stopifnot(all(vapply(panels, is.data.frame, logical(1L))))
  if (is.null(csv_df)) {
    csv_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
      lapply(seq_along(panels), function(idx) {
        dplyr::mutate(panels[[idx]], panel = panel_names[[idx]], .before = 1L)
      })
    )
  }
  write_table_csv_for_stub(csv_df, results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".csv")), file_stub, row_names = FALSE)
  render_table_blocks(
    definition_table_blocks(panels, panel_page_breaks = panel_page_breaks),
    title = caption,
    display_label = display_label,
    landscape = FALSE,
    page_break_before = page_break_before
  )
}

# helper: render a standalone table PDF that can be cited outside the notebook.
# This path is used for manuscript/supplement table artifacts with stable names.
resolve_latex_command <- function(cmd = "pdflatex") {
  cmd_path <- Sys.which(cmd)
  if (nzchar(cmd_path)) return(cmd_path)

  fallback <- fs::path(path.expand("~"), "Library", "TinyTeX", "bin", "universal-darwin", cmd)
  if (file.exists(fallback)) {
    tex_dir <- fs::path_dir(fallback)
    current_path <- Sys.getenv("PATH", unset = "")
    path_parts <- strsplit(current_path, .Platform$path.sep, fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    if (!tex_dir %in% path_parts) {
      Sys.setenv(PATH = paste(c(tex_dir, current_path), collapse = .Platform$path.sep))
    }
    return(fallback)
  }

  stop(
    "Required LaTeX command not found on PATH: ", cmd,
    ". Install TinyTeX or expose the TeX bin directory before rendering."
  )
}

write_standalone_table_pdf <- function(df, caption, file_stub,
                                       wide = FALSE,
                                       digits = 2,
                                       max_cols = 6,
                                       landscape = NULL,
                                       display_label = NULL,
                                       note = NULL) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df))
  out_csv <- results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".csv"))
  write_table_csv_for_stub(df, out_csv, file_stub, row_names = FALSE)

  if (is.null(landscape)) landscape <- (wide || ncol(df) > max_cols)
  n_cols <- ncol(df)
  parts <- if (n_cols <= max_cols) list(df) else {
    idx <- split(seq_len(n_cols), ceiling(seq_len(n_cols) / max_cols))
    lapply(idx, function(ii) df[, ii, drop = FALSE])
  }
  use_longtable <- nrow(df) > 40 && length(parts) == 1L && !isTRUE(landscape)

  build_kbl <- function(df_part) {
    kbl <- knitr::kable(
      df_part,
      format = "latex",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      longtable = use_longtable,
      caption = NULL,
      digits = digits
    )
    kbl <- kableExtra::kable_styling(
      kbl,
      latex_options = if (use_longtable) c("repeat_header") else c("hold_position", "scale_down"),
      font_size = if (ncol(df_part) > max_cols || wide) 6 else 7,
      full_width = FALSE,
      position = "center"
    )
    latex_safe_table_text(as.character(kbl))
  }

  letters_part <- LETTERS[seq_along(parts)]
  table_blocks <- unlist(lapply(seq_along(parts), function(i) {
    cap_i <- if (length(parts) == 1L) caption else paste0(caption, " (Part ", letters_part[i], ")")
    c(
      table_heading_block(cap_i, display_label = display_label),
      build_kbl(parts[[i]])
    )
  }))
  table_blocks <- c(table_blocks, table_note_block(note))
  if (isTRUE(landscape)) {
    table_blocks <- c("\\begin{landscape}", table_blocks, "\\end{landscape}")
  }

  tex_doc <- c(
    "\\documentclass{article}",
    "\\usepackage[margin=0.75in]{geometry}",
    "\\usepackage{booktabs}",
    "\\usepackage{longtable}",
    "\\usepackage{pdflscape}",
    "\\usepackage{float}",
    "\\usepackage{graphicx}",
    "\\usepackage{array}",
    "\\begin{document}",
    table_blocks,
    "\\end{document}"
  )

  build_dir <- fs::path(tempdir(), paste0("table_pdf_", file_stub))
  fs::dir_create(build_dir, recurse = TRUE)
  tex_file <- fs::path(build_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".tex"))
  writeLines(tex_doc, tex_file, useBytes = TRUE)

  latex_cmd <- resolve_latex_command("pdflatex")
  latex_log <- tryCatch(
    system2(
      latex_cmd,
      args = c(
        "-interaction=nonstopmode",
        "-halt-on-error",
        "-output-directory", build_dir,
        tex_file
      ),
      stdout = TRUE,
      stderr = TRUE
    ),
    error = function(e) stop("Standalone table PDF render failed for ", file_stub, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
  )
  latex_status <- attr(latex_log, "status")
  if (!is.null(latex_status) && latex_status != 0) {
    stop(
      "Standalone table PDF render failed for ", file_stub, " via pdflatex. Last log lines: ",
      paste(utils::tail(latex_log, 20), collapse = " | ")
    )
  }
  pdf_file <- sub("\\.tex$", ".pdf", tex_file)
  if (!file.exists(pdf_file)) {
    stop("Standalone table PDF render failed for ", file_stub, ": expected PDF not found at ", pdf_file)
  }
  out_pdf <- results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".pdf"))
  fs::file_copy(pdf_file, out_pdf, overwrite = TRUE)
  invisible(out_pdf)
}

write_standalone_definition_table_pdf <- function(panels, caption, file_stub,
                                                  display_label = NULL,
                                                  csv_df = NULL,
                                                  panel_page_breaks = TRUE) {
  stopifnot(is.list(panels), length(panels) > 0L)
  panel_names <- names(panels)
  if (is.null(panel_names) || any(!nzchar(panel_names))) {
    panel_names <- paste("Panel", LETTERS[seq_along(panels)])
  }
  stopifnot(all(vapply(panels, is.data.frame, logical(1L))))
  if (is.null(csv_df)) {
    csv_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
      lapply(seq_along(panels), function(idx) {
        dplyr::mutate(panels[[idx]], panel = panel_names[[idx]], .before = 1L)
      })
    )
  }
  write_table_csv_for_stub(csv_df, results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".csv")), file_stub, row_names = FALSE)

  table_blocks <- c(
    table_heading_block(caption, display_label = display_label),
    definition_table_blocks(panels, panel_page_breaks = panel_page_breaks, latex = TRUE)
  )
  tex_doc <- c(
    "\\documentclass{article}",
    "\\usepackage[margin=0.75in]{geometry}",
    "\\usepackage{array}",
    "\\begin{document}",
    table_blocks,
    "\\end{document}"
  )

  build_dir <- fs::path(tempdir(), paste0("table_pdf_", file_stub))
  fs::dir_create(build_dir, recurse = TRUE)
  tex_file <- fs::path(build_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".tex"))
  writeLines(tex_doc, tex_file, useBytes = TRUE)

  latex_cmd <- resolve_latex_command("pdflatex")
  latex_log <- tryCatch(
    system2(
      latex_cmd,
      args = c(
        "-interaction=nonstopmode",
        "-halt-on-error",
        "-output-directory", build_dir,
        tex_file
      ),
      stdout = TRUE,
      stderr = TRUE
    ),
    error = function(e) stop("Standalone definition table PDF render failed for ", file_stub, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
  )
  latex_status <- attr(latex_log, "status")
  if (!is.null(latex_status) && latex_status != 0) {
    stop(
      "Standalone definition table PDF render failed for ", file_stub, " via pdflatex. Last log lines: ",
      paste(utils::tail(latex_log, 20), collapse = " | ")
    )
  }
  pdf_file <- sub("\\.tex$", ".pdf", tex_file)
  if (!file.exists(pdf_file)) {
    stop("Standalone definition table PDF render failed for ", file_stub, ": expected PDF not found at ", pdf_file)
  }
  out_pdf <- results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".pdf"))
  fs::file_copy(pdf_file, out_pdf, overwrite = TRUE)
  invisible(out_pdf)
}

# helper: write a standalone sidecar PDF for a manuscript table that is rendered
# as explicit panels in the notebook PDF.
write_standalone_panel_table_pdf <- function(panels, caption, file_stub,
                                             digits = 2,
                                             display_label = NULL,
                                             landscape = TRUE,
                                             csv_df = NULL,
                                             panel_page_breaks = FALSE,
                                             scale_down = TRUE,
                                             font_size = 7,
                                             longtable = FALSE,
                                             column_widths = NULL,
                                             note = NULL) {
  stopifnot(is.list(panels), length(panels) > 0L)
  panel_names <- names(panels)
  if (is.null(panel_names) || any(!nzchar(panel_names))) {
    panel_names <- paste("Panel", LETTERS[seq_along(panels)])
  }
  stopifnot(all(vapply(panels, is.data.frame, logical(1L))))

  if (is.null(csv_df)) {
    csv_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
      lapply(seq_along(panels), function(idx) {
        dplyr::mutate(panels[[idx]], panel = panel_names[[idx]], .before = 1L)
      })
    )
  }
  write_table_csv_for_stub(csv_df, results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".csv")), file_stub, row_names = FALSE)

  build_kbl <- function(df_part) {
    kbl <- knitr::kable(
      df_part,
      format = "latex",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      longtable = longtable,
      digits = digits
    )
    latex_options <- if (isTRUE(longtable)) {
      c("repeat_header")
    } else {
      c("hold_position", if (isTRUE(scale_down)) "scale_down")
    }
    kbl <- kableExtra::kable_styling(
      kbl,
      latex_options = latex_options,
      font_size = font_size,
      full_width = FALSE,
      position = "center"
    )
    if (!is.null(column_widths)) {
      for (col_name in intersect(names(column_widths), names(df_part))) {
        kbl <- kableExtra::column_spec(
          kbl,
          which(names(df_part) == col_name),
          width = column_widths[[col_name]]
        )
      }
    }
    latex_safe_table_text(as.character(kbl))
  }

  table_blocks <- unlist(lapply(seq_along(panels), function(idx) {
    panel_title <- paste0("Panel ", LETTERS[[idx]], ". ", panel_names[[idx]])
    break_cmd <- if (isTRUE(panel_page_breaks) && idx > 1L) "\\clearpage" else character(0)
    c(
      break_cmd,
      paste0("\\medskip\\noindent\\textbf{", escape_latex_text(latex_safe_table_text(panel_title)), "}\\par"),
      build_kbl(panels[[idx]])
    )
  }))
  table_heading <- table_heading_block(caption, display_label = display_label)
  table_blocks <- c(table_blocks, table_note_block(note))
  if (isTRUE(landscape)) {
    table_blocks <- c("\\begin{landscape}", table_heading, table_blocks, "\\end{landscape}")
  } else {
    table_blocks <- c(table_heading, table_blocks)
  }

  tex_doc <- c(
    "\\documentclass{article}",
    "\\usepackage[margin=0.75in]{geometry}",
    "\\usepackage{booktabs}",
    "\\usepackage{pdflscape}",
    "\\usepackage{float}",
    "\\usepackage{graphicx}",
    "\\usepackage{array}",
    "\\begin{document}",
    table_blocks,
    "\\end{document}"
  )

  build_dir <- fs::path(tempdir(), paste0("table_pdf_", file_stub))
  fs::dir_create(build_dir, recurse = TRUE)
  tex_file <- fs::path(build_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".tex"))
  writeLines(tex_doc, tex_file, useBytes = TRUE)

  latex_cmd <- resolve_latex_command("pdflatex")
  latex_log <- tryCatch(
    system2(
      latex_cmd,
      args = c(
        "-interaction=nonstopmode",
        "-halt-on-error",
        "-output-directory", build_dir,
        tex_file
      ),
      stdout = TRUE,
      stderr = TRUE
    ),
    error = function(e) stop("Standalone panel table PDF render failed for ", file_stub, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
  )
  latex_status <- attr(latex_log, "status")
  if (!is.null(latex_status) && latex_status != 0) {
    stop(
      "Standalone panel table PDF render failed for ", file_stub, " via pdflatex. Last log lines: ",
      paste(utils::tail(latex_log, 20), collapse = " | ")
    )
  }
  pdf_file <- sub("\\.tex$", ".pdf", tex_file)
  if (!file.exists(pdf_file)) {
    stop("Standalone panel table PDF render failed for ", file_stub, ": expected PDF not found at ", pdf_file)
  }
  out_pdf <- results_path(paste0(file_stub, ".pdf"))
  fs::file_copy(pdf_file, out_pdf, overwrite = TRUE)
  invisible(out_pdf)
}

# helper: turn any gtsummary table into a PDF-safe LaTeX table while keeping
# numbering under explicit notebook control.
to_pdf_table <- function(tbl, font_size = 8, landscape = FALSE,
                         label_col_width = NULL, longtable = FALSE,
                         caption_mode = c("plain", "numbered", "none"),
                         display_label = NULL,
                         page_break_before = FALSE) {
  caption_mode <- match.arg(caption_mode)
  tbl_ncol <- tryCatch(ncol(gtsummary::as_tibble(tbl)), error = function(e) NA_integer_)
  if (is.finite(tbl_ncol) && tbl_ncol > 6 && font_size > 6) font_size <- 6

  caption_text <- tryCatch(tbl$table_styling$caption, error = function(e) NULL)
  tbl_local <- tbl
  if (!identical(caption_mode, "numbered")) {
    tbl_local$table_styling$caption <- NULL
  }

  kbl <- gtsummary::as_kable(
    tbl_local,
    format    = "latex",
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    longtable = longtable
  )

  if (!is.null(label_col_width)) {
    kbl <- kableExtra::column_spec(kbl, 1, width = label_col_width)
  }

  latex_opts <- if (longtable) {
    c("repeat_header")
  } else {
    c("hold_position", "scale_down")
  }
  kbl <- kableExtra::kable_styling(
    kbl,
    latex_options = latex_opts,
    font_size     = font_size,
    full_width    = !longtable,
    position      = "center"
  )

  kbl_txt <- latex_safe_table_text(as.character(kbl))
  if (identical(caption_mode, "numbered")) {
    mark_float_emitted()
    return(knitr::asis_output(kbl_txt))
  }
  title_i <- if (identical(caption_mode, "none")) NULL else caption_text
  render_table_blocks(
    kbl_txt,
    title = title_i,
    display_label = display_label,
    landscape = landscape,
    page_break_before = page_break_before
  )
}
```

Package dependencies are managed via `renv` for reproducibility. Use `renv::restore()` before rendering.

### Package Set Up

```{r setup-packages}
#| code-block-title: "Load required packages (renv-managed)"
#| include: false
#| results: "hide"
#| message: false
#| warning: false
# Consolidated package management ------------------------------------------------
seed_pkgs <- c(
  "Matrix", "WeightIt", "broom", "cobalt", "codebookr", "dagitty", "digest",
  "consort", "dplyr", "fastshap", "flextable", "fs", "future", "future.apply", "gbm",
  "ggdag", "ggplot2", "gt", "gtsummary", "haven", "here", "kableExtra",
  "knitr", "labelled", "lubridate", "mice", "miceadds", "mitools", "naniar",
  "officer", "parallel", "patchwork", "pdftools", "progressr", "purrr",
  "ragg", "renv", "rlang", "rms", "scales", "skimr", "survey",
  "tibble", "tidyr", "visdat"
)

# helper: scan the notebook source for package usage so the render fails fast
# if a collaborator has not restored the required `renv` environment.
extract_notebook_pkgs <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) return(character())
  lines <- readLines(path, warn = FALSE)
  # Local extractor used for both `library(pkg)` and `pkg::fn()` patterns.
  capture_group <- function(pattern, x) {
    hits <- regexec(pattern, x, perl = TRUE)
    matches <- regmatches(x, hits)
    vals <- vapply(matches, function(m) if (length(m) >= 2) m[2] else NA_character_, character(1))
    vals[is.finite(nchar(vals))]
  }
  lib_pkgs <- capture_group("library\\s*\\(\\s*['\"]?([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9.]*)['\"]?", lines)
  ns_pkgs <- capture_group("([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9.]*)::[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9._]*", lines)
  unique(c(lib_pkgs, ns_pkgs))
}

qmd_source <- here::here("Code Drafts", "ABG-VBG-analysis.qmd")
notebook_pkgs <- extract_notebook_pkgs(qmd_source)

renv_dep_pkgs <- character()
if ("renv" %in% rownames(installed.packages())) {
  renv_dep_pkgs <- tryCatch(
    {
      dep_tbl <- renv::dependencies(qmd_source, progress = FALSE)
      if (!is.null(dep_tbl$Package)) unique(dep_tbl$Package) else character()
    },
    error = function(e) character()
  )
}

base_drop <- c("base", "datasets", "graphics", "grDevices", "methods", "stats", "tools", "utils")
ignore_pkgs <- c("pkg")
required_pkgs <- sort(unique(setdiff(c(seed_pkgs, notebook_pkgs), c(base_drop, ignore_pkgs))))
if (length(renv_dep_pkgs)) {
  renv_only <- setdiff(renv_dep_pkgs, c(required_pkgs, base_drop, ignore_pkgs))
  if (length(renv_only)) {
    message("Dependency audit note (renv::dependencies only): ", paste(sort(renv_only), collapse = ", "))
  }
}

# Fail fast if packages are missing (use renv to install)
missing_pkgs <- setdiff(required_pkgs, rownames(installed.packages()))
if (length(missing_pkgs)) {
  stop(
    "Missing packages: ", paste(missing_pkgs, collapse = ", "),
    ". Install with renv::install(c(...)); if renv is absent, install 'renv' first; then run renv::snapshot()."
  )
}

# Attach packages from one authoritative location.
ensure_packages_loaded <- function(pkgs) {
  invisible(lapply(pkgs, function(pkg) {
    if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
      stop("Required package is not available: ", pkg)
    }
    if (!paste0("package:", pkg) %in% search()) {
      suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(pkg, character.only = TRUE))
    }
  }))
}
ensure_packages_loaded(required_pkgs)

# Centralize outputs under Results/, with paths relative to project root
data_dir_param <- Sys.getenv("ABGVBG_DATA_DIR", unset = params$data_dir)
results_dir_param <- Sys.getenv("ABGVBG_RESULTS_DIR", unset = params$results_dir)
data_dir_name <- fs::path_abs(data_dir_param, start = project_root)
results_dir <- fs::path_abs(results_dir_param, start = project_root)
if (!dir.exists(data_dir_name)) {
  stop("Data directory does not exist: ", data_dir_name)
}
fs::dir_create(results_dir, recurse = TRUE)
fig_dir      <- fs::path(results_dir, "figs")
fig_path     <- paste0(display_path(fig_dir, must_exist = FALSE), "/")
# helper: build paths under `Results/` from one canonical root so later code
# never has to guess where artifacts should be written.
results_path <- function(...) fs::path(results_dir, ...)
fs::dir_create(fig_dir, recurse = TRUE)
RENDER_EXTENDED_EXPLORATORY_SUPPLEMENT <- FALSE
RENDER_LIKELIHOOD_RATIO_SUPPLEMENT <- TRUE

# Static lineage metadata preserves the development chain without making the
# canonical notebook depend on superseded source files at render time. Hashes
# below describe the archived source states; the release-source hash is recorded
# separately in PROVENANCE.md after release preparation is complete.
notebook_lineage_metadata <- data.frame(
  sequence = seq_len(5L),
  source_role = c(
    "predecessor",
    "ratio_of_odds_ratios_predecessor",
    "stacked_ror_predecessor",
    "post_review_predecessor",
    "canonical_current"
  ),
  source_label = c(
    "ABG-VBG analysis 2026-5-6.qmd",
    "ABG-VBG analysis 2026-6-23 ratio-of-odds-ratios.qmd",
    "ABG-VBG analysis 2026-06-23 stacked-ror.qmd",
    "2026-06-26 ABG-VBG reverse-timing severity sensitivities.qmd",
    "ABG-VBG-analysis.qmd"
  ),
  recorded_sha256 = c(
    "ab6a006dd232c6780f6c3fb3b4d35da0bf11aaf88d0a8103f60dd7bfd1b5ce50",
    "5ef2448156f80e4106ab71c2c210d5df748beca2d23fc4fa4db5c8a2d427d618",
    "38620927eed1306b6e7655a63acd39b1920bcd1c78b3a991e081728305f19ba5",
    "2d8d93be8e93072dfa17d8a6d70cb7e54aa8a2d40b461c71f5bc68b163be2a0b",
    NA_character_
  ),
  runtime_requirement = "not_required",
  lineage_note = c(
    "Archived predecessor before ratio-of-odds-ratios work.",
    "Archived ratio-of-odds-ratios source state.",
    "Archived stacked-ROR source state.",
    "Post-review source state promoted to the stable canonical path.",
    "Single self-contained release source; final hash is recorded in PROVENANCE.md."
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
utils::write.csv(
  notebook_lineage_metadata,
  results_path("notebook_lineage_metadata.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE,
  na = ""
)

POSTER_DPI <- 300
POSTER_FIG1_WIDTH_IN <- 12.03
POSTER_FIG1_HEIGHT_IN <- 10.11
POSTER_FIG2_WIDTH_IN <- 19.15
POSTER_FIG2_HEIGHT_IN <- 12.50
POSTER_FONT_FAMILY <- "sans"

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.path   = fig_path,
  dev        = "ragg_png",
  dpi = 300
)
# on macOS and some setups this prevents device headaches
options(bitmapType = "cairo")
```

```{r cohort-flow-helpers}
#| include: false
#| results: "hide"
#| message: false
#| warning: false
# Purpose: cohort flow helpers.
format_count <- function(x) {
  format(as.integer(x), big.mark = ",", trim = TRUE, scientific = FALSE)
}

cohort_flow_input_path <- fs::path(data_dir_name, "full_db.dta")
cohort_flow_cols <- c("encounter_type", "has_abg", "has_vbg", "paco2", "vbg_co2")

# helper: read only the columns needed for Figure 1 so the cohort-flow section
# stays fast and does not depend on unrelated variables.
read_cohort_flow_input <- function(path, cols = cohort_flow_cols) {
  stopifnot(file.exists(path))
  haven::read_dta(path, col_select = dplyr::all_of(cols))
}

# helper: compute the count objects that feed the cohort-flow diagram. Keeping
# this logic in one function makes later figure edits less error-prone.
compute_cohort_flow_counts <- function(df) {
  encounter_type_num <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(df$encounter_type))
  if (anyNA(encounter_type_num)) {
    stop(
      "Cohort flow count input has missing encounter_type values (n = ",
      sum(is.na(encounter_type_num)), ")."
    )
  }

  has_abg_num <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(df$has_abg))
  has_vbg_num <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(df$has_vbg))
  has_abg_num[is.na(has_abg_num)] <- 0L
  has_vbg_num[is.na(has_vbg_num)] <- 0L

  analytic_flag <- encounter_type_num != 1L
  has_abg_flag <- analytic_flag & has_abg_num == 1L
  has_vbg_flag <- analytic_flag & has_vbg_num == 1L
  both_flag <- has_abg_flag & has_vbg_flag
  abg_only_flag <- has_abg_flag & !has_vbg_flag
  vbg_only_flag <- has_vbg_flag & !has_abg_flag
  neither_flag <- analytic_flag & !has_abg_flag & !has_vbg_flag
  abg_paco2_flag <- has_abg_flag & !is.na(df$paco2)
  vbg_co2_flag <- has_vbg_flag & !is.na(df$vbg_co2)

  analytic_n <- sum(analytic_flag, na.rm = TRUE)
  stopifnot(sum(abg_only_flag, na.rm = TRUE) + sum(vbg_only_flag, na.rm = TRUE) +
              sum(both_flag, na.rm = TRUE) + sum(neither_flag, na.rm = TRUE) == analytic_n)
  stopifnot(sum(abg_only_flag, na.rm = TRUE) + sum(both_flag, na.rm = TRUE) ==
              sum(has_abg_flag, na.rm = TRUE))
  stopifnot(sum(vbg_only_flag, na.rm = TRUE) + sum(both_flag, na.rm = TRUE) ==
              sum(has_vbg_flag, na.rm = TRUE))

  list(
    total_extracted = nrow(df),
    excluded_ambulatory = sum(encounter_type_num == 1L, na.rm = TRUE),
    analytic_ed_inpatient = analytic_n,
    abg_tested = sum(has_abg_flag, na.rm = TRUE),
    vbg_tested = sum(has_vbg_flag, na.rm = TRUE),
    both_abg_vbg = sum(both_flag, na.rm = TRUE),
    abg_only = sum(abg_only_flag, na.rm = TRUE),
    vbg_only = sum(vbg_only_flag, na.rm = TRUE),
    neither_abg_nor_vbg = sum(neither_flag, na.rm = TRUE),
    abg_with_paco2 = sum(abg_paco2_flag, na.rm = TRUE),
    vbg_with_vbg_co2 = sum(vbg_co2_flag, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# helper: fail early if the poster cohort schematic count identities no longer
# match the source-cohort design.
validate_cohort_flow_poster_counts <- function(counts) {
  required_names <- c(
    "total_extracted",
    "excluded_ambulatory",
    "analytic_ed_inpatient",
    "abg_tested",
    "vbg_tested",
    "both_abg_vbg",
    "abg_only",
    "vbg_only",
    "neither_abg_nor_vbg",
    "abg_with_paco2",
    "vbg_with_vbg_co2"
  )
  missing_names <- setdiff(required_names, names(counts))
  if (length(missing_names)) {
    stop(
      "Poster cohort-flow counts missing: ",
      paste(missing_names, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }
  count_vec <- vapply(counts[required_names], function(x) {
    as.numeric(x[[1]])
  }, numeric(1L))
  if (any(!is.finite(count_vec)) || any(count_vec < 0)) {
    stop("Poster cohort-flow counts must be finite non-negative values.")
  }
  if (count_vec[["abg_only"]] + count_vec[["both_abg_vbg"]] != count_vec[["abg_tested"]]) {
    stop("Poster cohort-flow counts do not reconcile: ABG-only + ABG+VBG != ABG-tested.")
  }
  if (count_vec[["vbg_only"]] + count_vec[["both_abg_vbg"]] != count_vec[["vbg_tested"]]) {
    stop("Poster cohort-flow counts do not reconcile: VBG-only + ABG+VBG != VBG-tested.")
  }
  overlap_total <- count_vec[["abg_only"]] + count_vec[["both_abg_vbg"]] +
    count_vec[["vbg_only"]] + count_vec[["neither_abg_nor_vbg"]]
  if (overlap_total != count_vec[["analytic_ed_inpatient"]]) {
    stop("Poster cohort-flow counts do not reconcile to the eligible source cohort.")
  }
  invisible(count_vec)
}

# helper: poster-only Figure 1 schematic emphasizing overlapping ABG/VBG
# analytic cohorts rather than a mutually exclusive consort-style split.
build_cohort_flow_poster_layout <- function(counts) {
  count_vec <- validate_cohort_flow_poster_counts(counts)
  boxes <- tibble::tribble(
    ~box_id, ~xmin, ~xmax, ~ymin, ~ymax, ~fill_key,
    "extracted", 1.6, 13.4, 16.0, 18.2, "source",
    "excluded", 14.2, 18.2, 14.6, 17.0, "excluded",
    "source", 1.6, 15.4, 11.0, 13.9, "source",
    "abg_only", 0.6, 4.6, 7.9, 10.0, "abg",
    "both", 4.6, 8.6, 7.9, 10.0, "overlap",
    "vbg_only", 8.6, 12.6, 7.9, 10.0, "vbg",
    "neither", 12.6, 16.6, 7.9, 10.0, "neither",
    "abg_analysis", 1.0, 7.2, 4.0, 6.7, "abg",
    "vbg_analysis", 10.0, 16.2, 4.0, 6.7, "vbg"
  )
  labels <- tibble::tribble(
    ~box_id, ~x, ~label_y, ~count_y, ~detail_y, ~label, ~count_label, ~detail,
    "extracted", 7.5, 17.55, 16.95, 16.35,
    "Extracted 2022 encounter cohort",
    paste0("n = ", format_count(count_vec[["total_extracted"]])),
    "Criteria for hypercapnia suspicion",
    "excluded", 16.2, 16.15, 15.35, NA_real_,
    "Ambulatory encounters\nexcluded",
    paste0("n = ", format_count(count_vec[["excluded_ambulatory"]])),
    NA_character_,
    "source", 8.5, 13.15, 12.35, NA_real_,
    "Eligible ED/inpatient source cohort",
    paste0("n = ", format_count(count_vec[["analytic_ed_inpatient"]])),
    NA_character_,
    "abg_only", 2.6, 9.35, 8.65, NA_real_,
    "ABG only",
    paste0("n = ", format_count(count_vec[["abg_only"]])),
    NA_character_,
    "both", 6.6, 9.35, 8.65, NA_real_,
    "ABG + VBG",
    paste0("n = ", format_count(count_vec[["both_abg_vbg"]])),
    NA_character_,
    "vbg_only", 10.6, 9.35, 8.65, NA_real_,
    "VBG only",
    paste0("n = ", format_count(count_vec[["vbg_only"]])),
    NA_character_,
    "neither", 14.6, 9.35, 8.65, NA_real_,
    "Neither test",
    paste0("n = ", format_count(count_vec[["neither_abg_nor_vbg"]])),
    NA_character_,
    "abg_analysis", 4.1, 6.05, 5.25, 4.55,
    "ABG Analysis cohort",
    paste0("n = ", format_count(count_vec[["abg_with_paco2"]])),
    "IPSW-standardized to source cohort",
    "vbg_analysis", 13.1, 6.05, 5.25, 4.55,
    "VBG Analysis cohort",
    paste0("n = ", format_count(count_vec[["vbg_with_vbg_co2"]])),
    "IPSW-standardized to source cohort"
  )
  branch_label <- tibble::tibble(
    x = 8.6,
    y = 10.55,
    label = "Blood gas ordered on day of admit"
  )
  arrows <- tibble::tribble(
    ~x, ~y, ~xend, ~yend,
    7.5, 16.0, 7.5, 13.9,
    13.4, 17.0, 14.2, 16.2,
    8.5, 11.0, 8.5, 10.0,
    2.6, 7.9, 4.1, 6.7,
    6.6, 7.9, 4.1, 6.7,
    6.6, 7.9, 13.1, 6.7,
    10.6, 7.9, 13.1, 6.7
  )
  list(
    boxes = boxes,
    labels = labels,
    branch_label = branch_label,
    arrows = arrows,
    xlim = c(0, 18.6),
    ylim = c(2.8, 18.6)
  )
}

audit_cohort_flow_poster_layout <- function(layout) {
  boxes <- layout$boxes
  xlim <- layout$xlim
  ylim <- layout$ylim
  pair_grid <- utils::combn(seq_len(nrow(boxes)), 2L)
  overlap_pairs <- apply(pair_grid, 2L, function(idx) {
    a <- boxes[idx[[1]], ]
    b <- boxes[idx[[2]], ]
    x_overlap <- min(a$xmax, b$xmax) - max(a$xmin, b$xmin)
    y_overlap <- min(a$ymax, b$ymax) - max(a$ymin, b$ymin)
    if (isTRUE(x_overlap > 0 && y_overlap > 0)) {
      paste(a$box_id, b$box_id, sep = " overlaps ")
    } else {
      NA_character_
    }
  })
  overlap_pairs <- stats::na.omit(overlap_pairs)
  outside_canvas <- boxes$box_id[
    boxes$xmin < xlim[[1]] | boxes$xmax > xlim[[2]] |
      boxes$ymin < ylim[[1]] | boxes$ymax > ylim[[2]]
  ]
  source_box <- boxes[boxes$box_id == "source", ]
  excluded_box <- boxes[boxes$box_id == "excluded", ]
  source_exclusion_x_gap <- max(
    excluded_box$xmin - source_box$xmax,
    source_box$xmin - excluded_box$xmax
  )
  source_exclusion_y_gap <- max(
    excluded_box$ymin - source_box$ymax,
    source_box$ymin - excluded_box$ymax
  )
  source_exclusion_separated <- source_exclusion_x_gap > 0 || source_exclusion_y_gap > 0
  tibble::tibble(
    check = c("figure1_box_overlap", "figure1_canvas_bounds", "figure1_source_exclusion_gap"),
    status = c(
      if (length(overlap_pairs)) "FAIL" else "PASS",
      if (length(outside_canvas)) "FAIL" else "PASS",
      if (isTRUE(source_exclusion_separated)) "PASS" else "FAIL"
    ),
    detail = c(
      if (length(overlap_pairs)) paste(overlap_pairs, collapse = "; ") else "No poster cohort-flow boxes overlap with positive area.",
      if (length(outside_canvas)) paste(outside_canvas, collapse = "; ") else "All poster cohort-flow boxes are inside the plotting canvas.",
      paste0(
        "Source/exclusion gaps: x = ", round(source_exclusion_x_gap, 2),
        ", y = ", round(source_exclusion_y_gap, 2), " coordinate units."
      )
    )
  )
}

build_cohort_flow_poster_plot <- function(counts) {
  poster_layout <- build_cohort_flow_poster_layout(counts)

  ggplot2::ggplot() +
    ggplot2::geom_segment(
      data = poster_layout$arrows,
      ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend),
      arrow = grid::arrow(length = grid::unit(0.14, "in"), type = "closed"),
      linewidth = 0.55,
      color = "grey35"
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_rect(
      data = poster_layout$boxes,
      ggplot2::aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = fill_key),
      color = "grey25",
      linewidth = 0.75
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(
      data = poster_layout$labels,
      ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = label_y, label = label),
      family = POSTER_FONT_FAMILY,
      fontface = "bold",
      size = 5.6,
      lineheight = 0.95,
      color = "grey10"
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(
      data = poster_layout$labels,
      ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = count_y, label = count_label),
      family = POSTER_FONT_FAMILY,
      fontface = "bold",
      size = 5.2,
      lineheight = 0.95,
      color = "grey10"
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(
      data = dplyr::filter(poster_layout$labels, !is.na(detail)),
      ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = detail_y, label = detail),
      family = POSTER_FONT_FAMILY,
      size = 4.2,
      lineheight = 0.95,
      color = "grey20"
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(
      data = poster_layout$branch_label,
      ggplot2::aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
      family = POSTER_FONT_FAMILY,
      fontface = "bold",
      size = 5.0,
      color = "grey20"
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        source = "#EAF2F6",
        excluded = "#F2E8DD",
        abg = "#DCEBFA",
        overlap = "#E6E2F4",
        vbg = "#F9E5CF",
        neither = "#ECECEC"
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::coord_equal(xlim = poster_layout$xlim, ylim = poster_layout$ylim, expand = FALSE) +
    ggplot2::theme_void(base_size = 18, base_family = POSTER_FONT_FAMILY) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(12, 12, 12, 12)
    )
}

# helper: add the shared comparability annotation beneath the two weighted
# endpoints without changing the underlying consort graph geometry.
decorate_weighted_comparability <- function(built_graph, weighted_nodes,
                                            label_text,
                                            text_gp = grid::gpar(col = "grey25", cex = 0.78),
                                            label_x = grid::unit(0.5, "npc"),
                                            label_y = grid::unit(0.035, "npc")) {
  stopifnot(inherits(built_graph, "gTree"))
  stopifnot(length(weighted_nodes) == 2L)

  band_label <- grid::textGrob(
    label = label_text,
    x = label_x,
    y = label_y,
    just = "center",
    gp = text_gp,
    name = "weighted_comparability_label"
  )

  list(
    built_graph = built_graph,
    pre_draw = NULL,
    post_draw = function() {
      grid::grid.draw(band_label)
    }
  )
}

# helper: export the cohort-flow graph to manuscript artifacts plus an optional
# low-DPI inline preview while drawing the same built grob on each device to
# avoid device-specific layout drift.
save_cohort_flow_outputs <- function(graph, png_file, pdf_file,
                                     width_mm = 180, height_mm = 150,
                                     dpi = 600, bg = "white",
                                     decorate_fn = NULL,
                                     preview_file = NULL,
                                     preview_dpi = 96) {
  width_in <- width_mm / 25.4
  height_in <- height_mm / 25.4
  draw_graph <- function(new_page = TRUE) {
    built_graph <- consort::build_grid(graph)
    decoration <- NULL
    if (!is.null(decorate_fn)) {
      decoration <- decorate_fn(built_graph)
      if (inherits(decoration, "gTree")) {
        built_graph <- decoration
        decoration <- NULL
      } else if (is.list(decoration) && inherits(decoration$built_graph, "gTree")) {
        built_graph <- decoration$built_graph
      }
    }
    # Raster devices and inline replay paths often need an explicit new page,
    # but a freshly opened PDF device already has page 1 active. Calling
    # grid.newpage() there creates a blank first page and pushes the diagram to
    # page 2, which LaTeX then includes incorrectly.
    if (isTRUE(new_page)) {
      grid::grid.newpage()
    }
    if (is.list(decoration) && is.function(decoration$pre_draw)) {
      decoration$pre_draw()
    }
    grid::grid.draw(built_graph)
    if (is.list(decoration) && is.function(decoration$post_draw)) {
      decoration$post_draw()
    }
  }

  fs::dir_create(fs::path_dir(png_file), recurse = TRUE)
  fs::dir_create(fs::path_dir(pdf_file), recurse = TRUE)

  ragg::agg_png(
    filename = png_file,
    width = width_mm,
    height = height_mm,
    units = "mm",
    res = dpi,
    background = bg
  )
  draw_graph(new_page = TRUE)
  grDevices::dev.off()

  if (capabilities("cairo")) {
    grDevices::cairo_pdf(
      filename = pdf_file,
      width = width_in,
      height = height_in,
      bg = bg
    )
  } else {
    grDevices::pdf(
      file = pdf_file,
      width = width_in,
      height = height_in,
      bg = bg
    )
  }
  draw_graph(new_page = FALSE)
  grDevices::dev.off()

  if (!is.null(preview_file)) {
    fs::dir_create(fs::path_dir(preview_file), recurse = TRUE)
    ragg::agg_png(
      filename = preview_file,
      width = width_mm,
      height = height_mm,
      units = "mm",
      res = preview_dpi,
      background = bg
    )
    draw_graph(new_page = TRUE)
    grDevices::dev.off()
  }

  stopifnot(file.exists(png_file), file.exists(pdf_file))
  if (!is.null(preview_file)) stopifnot(file.exists(preview_file))
  if (requireNamespace("pdftools", quietly = TRUE)) {
    pdf_pages <- tryCatch(pdftools::pdf_info(pdf_file)$pages, error = function(e) NA_integer_)
    if (is.finite(pdf_pages) && pdf_pages != 1L) {
      stop("Cohort flow PDF export should be 1 page, got ", pdf_pages, ".")
    }
  }
  invisible(list(png = png_file, pdf = pdf_file, preview = preview_file))
}

cohort_flow_input <- read_cohort_flow_input(cohort_flow_input_path)
cohort_flow_counts <- compute_cohort_flow_counts(cohort_flow_input)
rm(cohort_flow_input)
```

```{r cohort-flow-figure}
#| echo: true
#| message: false
#| warning: false
# Purpose: manuscript Figure 1 cohort flow figure.
flow_png <- results_path("cohort_flow.png")
flow_pdf <- results_path("cohort_flow.pdf")

top_box <- paste0(
  "Extracted 2022 encounter cohort\n",
  "(n = ", format_count(cohort_flow_counts$total_extracted), ")"
)

screening_side <- paste0(
  "Ambulatory encounters excluded\n",
  "(n = ", format_count(cohort_flow_counts$excluded_ambulatory), ")"
)

analytic_base_box <- paste0(
  "Analytic cohort: ED and Inpatient Encounters\n",
  "(n = ", format_count(cohort_flow_counts$analytic_ed_inpatient), ")"
)

split_boxes <- c(
  paste0(
    "ABG-tested encounters\n",
    "(n = ", format_count(cohort_flow_counts$abg_tested), ")"
  ),
  paste0(
    "VBG-tested encounters\n",
    "(n = ", format_count(cohort_flow_counts$vbg_tested), ")"
  )
)

weighted_box <- paste0(
  "ABG-weighted sample\n",
  "standardized to analytic cohort"
)
weighted_boxes <- c(
  weighted_box,
  paste0(
    "VBG-weighted sample\n",
    "standardized to analytic cohort"
  )
)

target_cohort_box_gp <- grid::gpar(fill = "grey96", col = "grey25", lwd = 1)

cohort_flow_graph <- consort::add_box(txt = top_box, text_width = 34) |>
  consort::add_side_box(txt = screening_side, text_width = 30) |>
  consort::add_box(
    txt = analytic_base_box,
    text_width = 34,
    box_gp = target_cohort_box_gp
  ) |>
  consort::add_split(txt = split_boxes, text_width = 24) |>
  consort::add_box(
    txt = weighted_boxes,
    text_width = 24,
    box_gp = target_cohort_box_gp
  )

weighted_nodes <- attr(cohort_flow_graph, "nodes.current")
stopifnot(length(weighted_nodes) == 2L)

save_cohort_flow_outputs(
  cohort_flow_graph,
  flow_png,
  flow_pdf,
  decorate_fn = function(built_graph) {
    decorate_weighted_comparability(
      built_graph,
      weighted_nodes = weighted_nodes,
      label_text = "Conditionally comparable after propensity weighting"
    )
  }
)
invisible(flow_png)
```

```{r setup-run-config}
#| include: false
# Purpose: setup run config.
stopifnot(!is.null(params$run_mode))
stopifnot(!is.null(params$pilot_frac))
stopifnot(!is.null(params$sample_seed))

RUN_MODE <- match.arg(tolower(params$run_mode), c("pilot", "full"))
PILOT_FRAC <- as.numeric(params$pilot_frac)
stopifnot(is.finite(PILOT_FRAC), PILOT_FRAC > 0, PILOT_FRAC <= 1)
IS_PILOT <- identical(RUN_MODE, "pilot")
if (IS_PILOT) {
  if (!(PILOT_FRAC > 0 && PILOT_FRAC < 1)) {
    stop("RUN_MODE='pilot' requires 0 < PILOT_FRAC < 1.")
  }
} else {
  if (!isTRUE(all.equal(PILOT_FRAC, 1))) {
    warning("RUN_MODE='full' ignores pilot_frac and forces PILOT_FRAC = 1.", call. = FALSE)
  }
  PILOT_FRAC <- 1
}
SAMPLE_SEED <- as.integer(params$sample_seed)
stopifnot(is.finite(SAMPLE_SEED))

pilot_frac <- PILOT_FRAC
FULL_RUN <- identical(RUN_MODE, "full")
MI_BATCH_THRESHOLD_PILOT <- 5000L
MI_BATCH_THRESHOLD_FULL <- 200000L
MI_BATCH_THRESHOLD <- if (IS_PILOT) MI_BATCH_THRESHOLD_PILOT else MI_BATCH_THRESHOLD_FULL
stopifnot(is.finite(MI_BATCH_THRESHOLD), MI_BATCH_THRESHOLD > 0)
PLOT_DROP_POLICY <- "warn"
PROB_EPS <- 1e-6
MAX_SHAPE_LEVELS <- 6L
SPLINE_GRID_N <- 200L
OR_XLIM <- c(0.25, 16)
TAIL_N_FINITE <- 5L
TAIL_WINDOW_ITERS <- 10L
BAL_XLIM_MAX <- 1.0
MC_ERR_RATIO_THRESH <- 0.10
MAX_LEVELS_GBM <- 50L
GBM_MM_EST_BYTES_WARN <- 6e9
MAX_GBM_MM_COLS_WARN <- 200L
MAX_GBM_MM_COLS_STOP <- 400L
MAX_GBM_FACTOR_LEVELS_WARN <- 30L
MAX_GBM_FACTOR_LEVELS_STOP <- 60L
MARGINAL_PREDICTION_CHUNK_ROWS <- 2500L

message(
  "CONFIG: RUN_MODE=", RUN_MODE,
  " | PILOT_FRAC=", PILOT_FRAC,
  " | SAMPLE_SEED=", SAMPLE_SEED
)
```

```{r setup-diagnostics-output}
#| include: false
# Purpose: setup diagnostics output.
diag_run_id <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
diag_run_ts <- as.character(Sys.time())
run_id <- diag_run_id
run_ts <- diag_run_ts
runtime_run_id <- diag_run_id
options(run_id = run_id, run_ts = run_ts)
internal_todo_path <- results_path(paste0("internal_todos_", diag_run_id, ".md"))
writeLines(INTERNAL_TODO_LINES, internal_todo_path, useBytes = TRUE)
runtime_log <- data.frame()
mi_warn_log <- data.frame(
  time = character(), stage = character(), component = character(),
  analysis_variant = character(), model_type = character(),
  group = character(), outcome = character(), imputation = integer(),
  batch = integer(), message = character(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
mi_info_log <- mi_warn_log
mi_outcome_diag <- data.frame()
memory_snapshots <- data.frame()
gbm_preflight_design_dims <- data.frame()
gbm_preflight_warnings <- data.frame()
factor_diag_written <- character()
plot_drop_log_df <- data.frame(
  plot = character(), reason = character(), n_dropped = integer(),
  n_before = integer(), n_after = integer(),
  stage = character(), extra = character(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# helper: clear diagnostics from prior renders so each notebook run writes a
# fresh, internally consistent set of audit outputs.
cleanup_diagnostics_outputs <- function() {
  diag_files <- c(
    "runtime_log.csv", "runtime_summary.csv", "runtime_summary_top15.csv",
    "warnings_log.csv", "mi_warnings_log.csv", "mi_info_log.csv",
    "diagnostics_summary.csv", "diagnostics_missingness.csv",
    "diagnostics_missingness-by-strata.csv",
    "balance_target_imp_summary.csv", "balance_target_by_imp.csv",
    "balance_target_worst_rows.csv", "balance_max_smd_by_imp.csv",
    "balance_worst_terms.csv", "balance_worst10.csv", "balance_table.csv",
    "weight_summary.csv", "ps_overlap_summary.csv",
    "model_fit_diagnostics.csv", "mi_outcome_fit_diagnostics.csv",
    "mi_fit_issue_summary.csv", "mi_m_stability.csv", "mi_maxit_sensitivity.csv",
    "mi_obs_vs_imp_summary.csv", "mi_spline_curve_abg.csv",
    "mi_spline_curve_vbg.csv",
    "mi_spline_prob_curve_abg.csv", "mi_spline_prob_curve_vbg.csv",
    "mi_spline_coef_abg.csv",
    "mi_spline_coef_vbg.csv", "diag-ps-shap-stability.csv",
    "ratio_of_odds_ratios_mi_ipw_cat3.csv",
    "ratio_of_odds_ratios_status.csv",
    "ratio_of_odds_ratios_mi_ipw_cat3_display.csv",
    "ror_legacy_independence_method_audit.csv",
    "stacked_ror_input_inventory.csv",
    "stacked_ror_id_availability_audit.csv",
    "stacked_ror_code_visibility_audit.csv",
    "stacked_ror_spline_readout_grid.csv",
    "stacked_ror_dataset_summary.csv",
    "stacked_ror_cluster_strategy.csv",
    "stacked_ror_fit_diagnostics.csv",
    "stacked_ror_by_imputation.csv",
    "stacked_ror_spline_by_imputation.csv",
    "stacked_ror_spline_local_interval_grid.csv",
    "stacked_ror_spline_local_interval_by_imputation.csv",
    "stacked_ror_spline_local_interval_5mm.csv",
    "stacked_ror_spline_local_interval_qc.csv",
    "stacked_ror_offset_scan_by_imputation.csv",
    "stacked_ror_offset_scan_summary_by_imputation.csv",
    "stacked_ror_offset_scan_summary.csv",
    "stacked_ror_offset_scan_support_audit.csv",
    "stacked_ror_offset_scan_validation.csv",
    "stacked_ror_offset_scan_display_qc.csv",
    "ror_figure_interpretability_qc.csv",
    "stacked_ror_pooled.csv",
    "stacked_ror_spline_readouts_5mm.csv",
    "stacked_ror_vs_table2_validation.csv",
    "stacked_ror_validation_status.csv",
    "table_s6_stacked_ror_cat3.csv",
    "table_s6_stacked_ror_cat3.pdf",
    "table_s7_stacked_ror_spline_readouts_5mm.csv",
    "table_s7_stacked_ror_spline_readouts_5mm.pdf",
    "table_s7_stacked_ror_spline_values.csv",
    "table_s7_stacked_ror_spline_values.pdf",
    "table_s8_offset_scan_summary.csv",
    "table_s8_offset_scan_summary.pdf",
    "figs/figure_s9_spline_ror_dual_axis.png",
    "figs/figure_s9_spline_ror_dual_axis.pdf",
    "figs/figure_s10_offset_scan_ror_trend.png",
    "figs/figure_s10_offset_scan_ror_trend.pdf",
    "mice_smoketest.log", "mice_batches_log.csv", "mice_chain_diagnostics.csv",
    "mice_pred_width_preflight.csv", "mice_logged_events_raw.csv",
    "mice_logged_events_summary.csv", "mice_spec.rds",
    "missingness-by-strata.csv", "missingness-drivers.csv",
    "missingness-pattern.csv", "plot_drop_log.csv",
    "plot_registry.csv",
    "mi_logistic_ps_covariate_types.csv",
    "mi_logistic_ps_abg_list.rds",
    "mi_logistic_ps_vbg_list.rds",
    "mi_single_pass_status.csv", "mi_single_pass_cache_manifest.csv",
    "mi_single_pass_memory_log.csv", "mi_single_pass_storage_equivalence.csv"
  )
  diag_patterns <- c(
    "^mice_.*\\.(csv|log|rds|txt)$",
    "^diagnostics_.*\\.csv$",
    "^runtime_.*\\.csv$",
    "^warnings_.*\\.csv$",
    "^balance_.*\\.csv$",
    "^weight_summary\\.csv$",
    "^ps_overlap_summary\\.csv$",
    "^model_fit_diagnostics\\.csv$",
    "^plot_drop_log\\.csv$",
    "^diag-.*\\.csv$",
    "^mi_logistic_ps_.*\\.(csv|rds)$",
    "^mi_single_pass_.*\\.csv$"
  )
  files <- list.files(results_dir, full.names = TRUE)
  base <- basename(files)
  match_files <- base %in% diag_files
  if (length(diag_patterns)) {
    match_files <- match_files | grepl(paste(diag_patterns, collapse = "|"), base)
  }
  to_delete <- files[match_files]
  if (length(to_delete)) {
    safe_root <- normalizePath(results_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
    del_paths <- normalizePath(to_delete, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
    stopifnot(all(startsWith(del_paths, safe_root)))
  }
  fs::file_delete(to_delete)

  diag_figs <- list.files(fig_dir, full.names = TRUE)
  diag_figs <- diag_figs[grepl("^(diag-|or-plot|ps-)|-report-preview\\.png$", basename(diag_figs))]
  if (length(diag_figs)) {
    safe_root <- normalizePath(results_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
    del_paths <- normalizePath(diag_figs, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
    stopifnot(all(startsWith(del_paths, safe_root)))
  }
  fs::file_delete(diag_figs)
  {
    d <- fs::path(results_dir, "mice_batches")
    if (dir.exists(d)) fs::dir_delete(d)
  }
  {
    d <- fs::path(results_dir, "diagnostics_audit_snippets")
    if (dir.exists(d)) fs::dir_delete(d)
  }
  {
    f <- fs::path(results_dir, "mi_partial_mids.rds")
    if (file.exists(f)) fs::file_delete(f)
  }
}

# helper: remove heavyweight MI scratch artifacts after successful renders so
# routine notebook runs do not silently accumulate stale intermediate objects.
cleanup_mi_transient_outputs <- function() {

  transient_files <- c(
    results_path("mi_abg_vbg_mids.rds"),
    results_path("subset_data_pre_mi.rds"),
    results_path("mi_logistic_ps_abg_list.rds"),
    results_path("mi_logistic_ps_vbg_list.rds")
  )
  transient_dirs <- c(
    results_path("mi_weights"),
    results_path("mi_fit_cache")
  )

  existing_files <- transient_files[file.exists(transient_files)]
  if (length(existing_files)) {
    safe_root <- normalizePath(results_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
    del_paths <- normalizePath(existing_files, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
    stopifnot(all(startsWith(del_paths, safe_root)))
    fs::file_delete(existing_files)
  }

  existing_dirs <- transient_dirs[dir.exists(transient_dirs)]
  if (length(existing_dirs)) {
    safe_root <- normalizePath(results_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
    del_paths <- normalizePath(existing_dirs, winslash = "/", mustWork = FALSE)
    stopifnot(all(startsWith(del_paths, safe_root)))
    fs::dir_delete(existing_dirs)
  }

  invisible(length(existing_files) + length(existing_dirs) > 0L)
}

cleanup_diagnostics_outputs()
cleanup_mi_transient_outputs()
# Ensure any stale Results artifacts from prior runs are removed
stale_patterns <- c(
  "^missingness_by_strata\\.csv$",
  "^missingness-drivers\\.csv$",
  "^missingness_by_strata_preview\\.csv$",
  "^missingness_drivers_top20\\.csv$",
  "^missingness_top10\\.csv$",
  "^outcome_counts_by_cohort\\.csv$",
  "^weighting_diagnostics_non_mi\\.csv$",
  "^mi_specification\\.csv$",
  "^memory_snapshots\\.csv$",
  "^gbm_preflight_design_dims\\.csv$",
  "^gbm_preflight_factor_levels_.*\\.csv$",
  "^diag_mi_shap_errors\\.csv$",
  "^diag_mi_shap_method_used\\.csv$",
  "^mi_shap_imp_diagnostics\\.csv$",
  "^mi_logistic_ps_factor_levels_.*\\.csv$",
  "^mi_logistic_ps_.*\\.(csv|rds)$",
  "^get_imp_usage\\.csv$",
  "^diagnostics_audit\\.md$",
  "^pdf_page_text_stats\\.csv$",
  "^pdf_blank_pages\\.json$",
  "^pdf_hygiene_scan\\.csv$",
  "^publication_quality_asset_audit\\.csv$",
  "^publication_quality_asset_audit_summary\\.csv$",
  "^publication_quality_asset_audit\\.md$",
  "^publication_quality_pdf_text_scan\\.csv$",
  "^poster_visual_qc\\.md$",
  "^poster_caption_text\\.md$",
  "^table_visual_qc\\.csv$",
  "^table_visual_qc\\.md$",
  "^pdf_parse_table_figure_check\\.csv$",
  "^prognostic_likelihood_ratio_pdf_parse_check\\.csv$",
  "^figure2_probability_standardization_audit\\.md$",
  "^gbm_probability_standardization_audit\\.md$",
  "^prognostic_likelihood_ratio_.*\\.(csv|md|pdf)$",
  "^table_s1[2-5]_.*\\.(csv|pdf)$"
)
stale_files <- list.files(results_dir, full.names = TRUE)
stale_base <- basename(stale_files)
stale_files <- stale_files[grepl(paste(stale_patterns, collapse = "|"), stale_base)]
if (length(stale_files)) fs::file_delete(stale_files)

obsolete_fig_patterns <- c(
  "^figure_s13_spline_prognostic_likelihood_ratios(-report-preview)?\\.(png|pdf|svg)$",
  "^figure_s14_offset_scan_prognostic_likelihood_ratio(-report-preview)?\\.(png|pdf|svg)$"
)
obsolete_figs <- list.files(fig_dir, full.names = TRUE)
obsolete_fig_base <- basename(obsolete_figs)
obsolete_figs <- obsolete_figs[grepl(paste(obsolete_fig_patterns, collapse = "|"), obsolete_fig_base)]
if (length(obsolete_figs)) fs::file_delete(obsolete_figs)
```

## Helper functions for model diagnostics

```{r setup-diagnostics-helpers}
#| code-block-title: "Diagnostics helpers"
#| include: false
#| echo: true
#| message: false
#| warning: false
# Diagnostics helper functions for MI/IPW pipeline

# helper: fail early if an analysis stage expects complete covariates but the
# current data still contains missing values in those columns.
assert_no_na_covars <- function(df, covars, context = "") {
  missing <- setdiff(covars, names(df))
  stopifnot(length(missing) == 0)
  na_counts <- vapply(df[, covars, drop = FALSE], function(x) sum(is.na(x)), numeric(1))
  if (any(na_counts > 0)) {
    msg <- paste0(
      "NA values found in covariates (", context, "): ",
      paste(names(na_counts)[na_counts > 0], na_counts[na_counts > 0], collapse = ", ")
    )
    stop(msg)
  }
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: collapse the raw `mice` logged-events table into counts that are
# easier to review in diagnostics CSVs and notebook output.
summarize_logged_events <- function(mids_obj) {
  ev <- mids_obj$loggedEvents
  if (is.null(ev) || nrow(ev) == 0L) {
    return(data.frame(
      variable = character(),
      method = character(),
      event = character(),
      n = integer(),
      pct = numeric(),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  stopifnot(all(c("dep", "meth", "out") %in% names(ev)))
  tbl <- as.data.frame(table(ev$dep, ev$meth, ev$out), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  names(tbl) <- c("variable", "method", "event", "n")
  tbl <- tbl[tbl$n > 0, , drop = FALSE]
  tbl <- tbl[order(-tbl$n), , drop = FALSE]
  tbl$pct <- tbl$n / sum(tbl$n)
  tbl
}

# helper: consistently pull propensity scores out of WeightIt objects.
extract_weightit_ps <- function(w) {
  stopifnot(!is.null(w$ps))
  as.numeric(w$ps)
}

# helper: effective sample size for a vector of weights.
ess <- function(w) {
  w <- w[is.finite(w)]
  stopifnot(length(w) > 0)
  sum(w)^2 / sum(w^2)
}

# helper: quantify how much total weight is concentrated in the largest weights.
weight_concentration <- function(w, top_p = 0.01) {
  w <- w[is.finite(w)]
  stopifnot(length(w) > 0)
  ord <- sort(w, decreasing = TRUE)
  n_top <- max(1L, floor(length(ord) * top_p))
  sum(ord[seq_len(n_top)]) / sum(ord)
}

# helper: summarize one set of inverse-probability weights and their matching
# propensity scores for later diagnostics tables.
weight_summary <- function(w, ps, ps_floor, truncated) {
  w_ok <- w[is.finite(w)]
  stopifnot(length(w_ok) > 0)
  ps_ok <- ps[is.finite(ps)]
  stopifnot(length(ps_ok) > 0)
  out <- data.frame(
    n = length(w_ok),
    mean = mean(w_ok, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = stats::sd(w_ok, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(w_ok, na.rm = TRUE),
    p01 = stats::quantile(w_ok, 0.01, na.rm = TRUE),
    p05 = stats::quantile(w_ok, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    p95 = stats::quantile(w_ok, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
    p99 = stats::quantile(w_ok, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(w_ok, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum_w = sum(w_ok, na.rm = TRUE),
    ess = ess(w_ok),
    top01_weight_share = weight_concentration(w_ok, top_p = 0.01),
    ps_floor = ps_floor,
    trunc_rate = mean(truncated, na.rm = TRUE),
    ps_min = min(ps_ok, na.rm = TRUE),
    ps_p01 = stats::quantile(ps_ok, 0.01, na.rm = TRUE),
    ps_p05 = stats::quantile(ps_ok, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
    ps_p95 = stats::quantile(ps_ok, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
    ps_max = max(ps_ok, na.rm = TRUE),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  out
}

# helper: convert fitted propensities into the project-specific one-sided IPSW
# weights used to standardize the tested cohorts back to the analytic cohort.
compute_ipow_weights <- function(w, treat, ps_floor_quantile = 0.01,
                                 stabilize = TRUE) {
  # One-sided IPSW for this project:
  # outcome models are fit only within the tested cohort, so only tested rows
  # receive inverse-propensity weights. The target is the full analytic cohort,
  # not the untested subgroup. We truncate extremely small propensities to avoid
  # unstable huge weights, then optionally stabilize so treated weights average ~1.
  ps <- extract_weightit_ps(w)
  if (length(ps) != length(treat)) stop("Propensity length mismatch.")
  treat <- as.integer(treat)
  if (!all(treat %in% c(0L, 1L))) stop("Treatment indicator must be 0/1.")

  ps_obs <- ps[treat == 1L & is.finite(ps)]
  if (!length(ps_obs)) stop("No treated observations with finite propensity.")
  ps_floor <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(ps_obs, probs = ps_floor_quantile, na.rm = TRUE))
  if (!is.finite(ps_floor) || ps_floor <= 0) stop("Invalid propensity floor.")

  w_raw <- rep(NA_real_, length(ps))
  w_raw[treat == 1L] <- 1 / ps[treat == 1L]

  cap <- 1 / ps_floor
  truncated <- treat == 1L & is.finite(ps) & ps < ps_floor
  w_trunc <- w_raw
  w_trunc[truncated] <- cap

  if (stabilize) {
    w_trunc <- w_trunc / mean(w_trunc[treat == 1L], na.rm = TRUE)
  }

  list(
    weights   = w_trunc,
    ps        = ps,
    ps_floor  = ps_floor,
    cap       = cap,
    truncated = truncated
  )
}

# helper: stop immediately if a weighting step produces NaN/Inf values.
assert_finite_weights <- function(w, name = "weights") {
  if (any(!is.finite(w))) {
    stop("Non-finite values detected in ", name, ".")
  }
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: time an expression and append one standardized row to the runtime log.
runtime_logger <- function(step_name, expr, notes = NA_character_) {
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  result <- eval(expr)
  end_time <- Sys.time()
  sec <- as.numeric(difftime(end_time, start_time, units = "secs"))
  row <- data.frame(
    step_name = step_name,
    seconds = sec,
    start_time = as.character(start_time),
    end_time = as.character(end_time),
    notes = notes,
    run_id = runtime_run_id,
    run_mode = RUN_MODE,
    n_subset = subset_n,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  runtime_log <<- dplyr::bind_rows(runtime_log, row)
  result
}

# helper: create a consistently labeled context object so warnings and model
# diagnostics can be traced back to the exact analysis stage that produced them.
make_context <- function(stage, component,
                         analysis_variant = NA_character_,
                         model_type = NA_character_,
                         group = NA_character_,
                         outcome = NA_character_,
                         imputation = NA_integer_,
                         batch = NA_integer_) {
  list(
    stage = stage,
    component = component,
    analysis_variant = analysis_variant,
    model_type = model_type,
    group = group,
    outcome = outcome,
    imputation = imputation,
    batch = batch
  )
}

# helper: evaluate an expression while intercepting warnings into a structured
# data frame instead of letting them vanish into console output.
capture_warnings <- function(expr, context) {
  warn_rows <- list()
  stopifnot(all(c("stage", "component", "analysis_variant", "model_type",
                  "group", "outcome", "imputation", "batch") %in% names(context)))
  ctx <- list(
    stage = as.character(context$stage),
    component = as.character(context$component),
    analysis_variant = as.character(context$analysis_variant),
    model_type = as.character(context$model_type),
    group = as.character(context$group),
    outcome = as.character(context$outcome),
    imputation = as.integer(context$imputation),
    batch = as.integer(context$batch)
  )
  val <- withCallingHandlers(
    expr,
    warning = function(w) {
      warn_rows <<- append(warn_rows, list(data.frame(
        time = as.character(Sys.time()),
        stage = ctx$stage,
        component = ctx$component,
        analysis_variant = ctx$analysis_variant,
        model_type = ctx$model_type,
        group = ctx$group,
        outcome = ctx$outcome,
        imputation = ctx$imputation,
        batch = ctx$batch,
        message = conditionMessage(w),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )))
      invokeRestart("muffleWarning")
    }
  )
  warnings_df <- if (length(warn_rows)) {
```
