    dplyr::bind_rows(warn_rows)
  } else {
    data.frame(
      time = character(), stage = character(), component = character(),
      analysis_variant = character(), model_type = character(),
      group = character(), outcome = character(), imputation = integer(),
      batch = integer(), message = character(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
  list(value = val, warnings = warnings_df)
}

# helper: append captured warnings to the global MI warning logs, while
# separating informational `mice` messages from actionable warnings.
append_warnings <- function(wlist) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(wlist) || is.list(wlist))
  df <- if (is.data.frame(wlist)) wlist else dplyr::bind_rows(wlist)
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df))
  stopifnot("message" %in% names(df))
  info_rows <- df[grepl("^Number of logged events", df$message), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(info_rows)) {
    mi_info_log <<- dplyr::bind_rows(mi_info_log, info_rows)
  }
  df <- df[!grepl("^Number of logged events", df$message), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(df) == 0L) return(invisible(FALSE))
  mi_warn_log <<- dplyr::bind_rows(mi_warn_log, df)
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: resolve the active notebook path across Quarto/knitr working-directory
# changes so later audits can always locate the source file.
resolve_current_qmd <- function() {
  # Resolve qmd path robustly across render/knit working directories.
  input_candidates <- c(
    tryCatch(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE), error = function(e) NA_character_),
    tryCatch(knitr::current_input(), error = function(e) NA_character_)
  )
  input_candidates <- input_candidates[!is.na(input_candidates) & nzchar(input_candidates)]
  input_qmd_candidates <- unique(c(
    input_candidates[grepl("\\.qmd$", input_candidates, ignore.case = TRUE)],
    sub("\\.rmarkdown$", ".qmd", input_candidates, ignore.case = TRUE),
    here::here(
      "Code Drafts",
      sub("\\.rmarkdown$", ".qmd", basename(input_candidates), ignore.case = TRUE)
    )
  ))
  input_qmd_candidates <- input_qmd_candidates[
    grepl("\\.qmd$", input_qmd_candidates, ignore.case = TRUE)
  ]
  candidates <- c(
    input_qmd_candidates,
    here::here("Code Drafts", "ABG-VBG-analysis.qmd"),
    "Code Drafts/ABG-VBG-analysis.qmd",
    "ABG-VBG-analysis.qmd"
  )
  candidates <- candidates[!is.na(candidates) & nzchar(candidates)]
  existing <- candidates[file.exists(candidates)]
  if (!length(existing)) {
    stop("Could not resolve qmd source path for audit checks.")
  }
  normalizePath(existing[[1]], winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)
}

# helper: pull warning tables out of lists of fit results and append them to
# the notebook-wide warning logs in one call.
collect_warnings_from_list <- function(xlist) {
  warn_list <- lapply(xlist, function(x) {
    if (is.list(x) && !is.null(x$warnings)) x$warnings else NULL
  })
  warn_list <- warn_list[!vapply(warn_list, is.null, logical(1))]
  append_warnings(warn_list)
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: accumulate per-model outcome diagnostics into one project-wide table.
append_outcome_diag <- function(df) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df))
  if (nrow(df) == 0L) return(invisible(FALSE))
  mi_outcome_diag <<- dplyr::bind_rows(mi_outcome_diag, df)
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: collapse the warnings log to a concise count table for review.
summarize_warnings_log <- function(path = results_path("warnings_log.csv")) {
  warn_df <- read.csv(path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  if (nrow(warn_df) == 0L) return(data.frame())
  warn_df |>
    dplyr::count(stage, component, analysis_variant, model_type, message, sort = TRUE)
}

# helper: write CSV outputs with run identifiers attached so later audits can
# tell which render produced a given artifact.
write_csv_safely <- function(df, path, row_names = FALSE, required_cols = NULL,
                             include_run_metadata = TRUE) {
  run_id <- diag_run_id
  run_ts <- diag_run_ts
  stopifnot(!is.null(df))
  df <- as.data.frame(df)
  # Normalize pillar/vctrs numeric classes to base numeric for CSV export
  df <- dplyr::mutate(
    df,
    dplyr::across(
      where(~ inherits(.x, "pillar_num")),
      ~ suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.x))
    )
  )
  if (!is.null(required_cols)) {
    missing <- setdiff(required_cols, names(df))
    stopifnot(length(missing) == 0)
  }
  if (nrow(df) == 0L) {
    if (!("empty" %in% names(df))) df$empty <- logical(0)
  }
  if (isTRUE(include_run_metadata)) {
    df$run_id <- rep(run_id, nrow(df))
    df$run_ts <- rep(run_ts, nrow(df))
  }
  fs::dir_create(dirname(path))
  tmp_path <- tempfile(
    pattern = paste0(".", fs::path_file(path), "."),
    tmpdir = dirname(path),
    fileext = ".tmp"
  )
  ok <- FALSE
  on.exit({
    if (!ok && file.exists(tmp_path)) {
      unlink(tmp_path, force = TRUE)
    }
  }, add = TRUE)
  utils::write.csv(df, tmp_path, row.names = row_names)
  ok <- file.rename(tmp_path, path)
  if (!isTRUE(ok)) {
    file.copy(tmp_path, path, overwrite = TRUE)
    ok <- file.exists(path)
  }
  if (!isTRUE(ok)) {
    stop("Failed to move temporary CSV into place: ", path, call. = FALSE)
  }
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: write line-based diagnostics with the same run metadata header used
# for CSV artifacts.
write_diag_lines <- function(lines, path) {
  run_id <- diag_run_id
  run_ts <- diag_run_ts
  header <- paste0("run_id: ", run_id, " run_ts: ", run_ts)
  writeLines(c(header, lines), con = path)
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: drop unused factor levels after filtering so model frames and tables
# do not silently carry dead categories.
droplevels_all <- function(df, vars = NULL) {
  if (is.null(vars)) {
    vars <- names(df)[vapply(df, is.factor, logical(1))]
  } else {
    vars <- intersect(vars, names(df))
    vars <- vars[vapply(df[vars], is.factor, logical(1))]
  }
  for (nm in vars) df[[nm]] <- droplevels(df[[nm]])
  df
}

# helper: append one GC-based memory snapshot for a named stage. This makes it
# easier to identify where long renders begin to consume disproportionate RAM.
append_mem_snapshot <- function(stage_id, context_id = NA_character_, when = "pre") {
  g <- gc()
  cn <- colnames(g)
  used_col  <- if ("used" %in% cn) "used" else cn[grepl("used", cn)][1]
  trig_col  <- if ("gc trigger" %in% cn) "gc trigger" else cn[grepl("trigger", cn)][1]
  max_col   <- if ("max used" %in% cn) "max used" else cn[grepl("max", cn)][1]
  n_used <- as.numeric(g["Ncells", used_col])
  v_used <- as.numeric(g["Vcells", used_col])
  v_trig <- as.numeric(g["Vcells", trig_col])
  v_max  <- as.numeric(g["Vcells", max_col])
  mem_max <- tryCatch(mem.maxVSize(), error = function(e) NA_real_)
  row <- data.frame(
    run_id = diag_run_id,
    run_ts = diag_run_ts,
    stage_id = stage_id,
    context_id = context_id,
    when = when,
    time = as.character(Sys.time()),
    Ncells_used = n_used,
    Vcells_used = v_used,
    Vcells_gc_trigger = v_trig,
    Vcells_max_used = v_max,
    mem_max_vsize = mem_max,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  memory_snapshots <<- dplyr::bind_rows(memory_snapshots, row)
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: write a compact CSV describing factor cardinality before expensive
# GBM fits, since high-cardinality factors are a common failure mode.
write_factor_levels_diag <- function(df, vars, context_id, file_prefix = "gbm_preflight_factor_levels") {
  key <- paste0(file_prefix, "::", context_id)
  if (key %in% factor_diag_written) {
    return(invisible(FALSE))
  }
  vars <- intersect(vars, names(df))
  if (!length(vars)) {
    return(invisible(FALSE))
  }
  diag <- lapply(vars, function(v) {
    x <- df[[v]]
    if (!is.factor(x)) return(NULL)
    tab <- table(x, useNA = "ifany")
    n_unused <- sum(tab == 0)
    top <- utils::head(sort(tab, decreasing = TRUE), 5)
    data.frame(
      variable = v,
      class = class(x)[1],
      nlevels = nlevels(x),
      n_unused_levels = n_unused,
      top_levels = paste(names(top), as.integer(top), sep = "=", collapse = "; "),
      n_missing = sum(is.na(x)),
      context_id = context_id,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })
  diag <- dplyr::bind_rows(diag)
  if (nrow(diag) == 0L) return(invisible(FALSE))
  out_path <- results_path(paste0(file_prefix, "_", context_id, ".csv"))
  write_csv_safely(diag, out_path, row_names = FALSE)
  factor_diag_written <<- c(factor_diag_written, key)
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: estimate sampled design-matrix width so large GBM model matrices can
# be anticipated before the full fit is attempted.
log_design_dims <- function(df, covars, context_id, sample_n = 5000L) {
  covars <- intersect(covars, names(df))
  if (!length(covars)) return(invisible(FALSE))
  n_full <- nrow(df)
  n_samp <- min(sample_n, n_full)
  set.seed(20251206)
  idx <- sample.int(n_full, n_samp)
  df_s <- df[idx, covars, drop = FALSE]
  fml <- stats::as.formula(paste("~", paste(covars, collapse = " + ")))
  mm <- stats::model.matrix(fml, data = df_s)
  n_factor <- sum(vapply(df_s, is.factor, logical(1)))
  max_nlevels <- if (n_factor > 0) max(vapply(df_s[vapply(df_s, is.factor, logical(1))], nlevels, integer(1))) else 0L
  row <- data.frame(
    run_id = diag_run_id,
    run_ts = diag_run_ts,
    context_id = context_id,
    nrow_full = n_full,
    nrow_sample = n_samp,
    ncol_model_matrix = ncol(mm),
    n_factor_vars = n_factor,
    max_nlevels_factor = max_nlevels,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  gbm_preflight_design_dims <<- dplyr::bind_rows(gbm_preflight_design_dims, row)
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: run the full GBM preflight suite: drop empty levels, estimate design
# size, log warnings, and stop when the model matrix looks infeasible.
gbm_preflight <- function(df, covars, context_id, sample_n = 50000L) {
  df <- droplevels_all(df)
  write_factor_levels_diag(df, covars, context_id, file_prefix = "gbm_preflight_factor_levels")
  covars <- intersect(covars, names(df))
  if (!length(covars)) return(invisible(FALSE))
  n_full <- nrow(df)
  n_samp <- min(sample_n, n_full)
  set.seed(20251206)
  idx <- sample.int(n_full, n_samp)
  df_s <- df[idx, covars, drop = FALSE]
  fml <- stats::as.formula(paste("~", paste(covars, collapse = " + ")))
  mm <- stats::model.matrix(fml, data = df_s)
  n_factor <- sum(vapply(df_s, is.factor, logical(1)))
  max_nlevels <- if (n_factor > 0) {
    max(vapply(df_s[vapply(df_s, is.factor, logical(1))], nlevels, integer(1)))
  } else 0L
  est_bytes <- n_full * ncol(mm) * 8
  row <- data.frame(
    run_id = diag_run_id,
    run_ts = diag_run_ts,
    context_id = context_id,
    nrow_full = n_full,
    nrow_sample = n_samp,
    ncol_model_matrix = ncol(mm),
    n_factor_vars = n_factor,
    max_nlevels_factor = max_nlevels,
    est_bytes = est_bytes,
    est_gb = est_bytes / 1024^3,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  gbm_preflight_design_dims <<- dplyr::bind_rows(gbm_preflight_design_dims, row)
  warn_msgs <- character()
  if (is.finite(est_bytes) && est_bytes > GBM_MM_EST_BYTES_WARN) {
    warn_msgs <- c(warn_msgs, paste0("est_dense_mm_gb>", round(GBM_MM_EST_BYTES_WARN / 1024^3, 2)))
  }
  if (ncol(mm) > MAX_GBM_MM_COLS_WARN) {
    warn_msgs <- c(warn_msgs, paste0("mm_cols_warn>", MAX_GBM_MM_COLS_WARN))
  }
  if (max_nlevels > MAX_GBM_FACTOR_LEVELS_WARN) {
    warn_msgs <- c(warn_msgs, paste0("factor_levels_warn>", MAX_GBM_FACTOR_LEVELS_WARN))
  }
  if (length(warn_msgs)) {
    gbm_preflight_warnings <<- dplyr::bind_rows(
      gbm_preflight_warnings,
      data.frame(
        run_id = diag_run_id,
        run_ts = diag_run_ts,
        context_id = context_id,
        nrow_full = n_full,
        ncol_model_matrix = ncol(mm),
        max_nlevels_factor = max_nlevels,
        est_gb = est_bytes / 1024^3,
        warning = paste(warn_msgs, collapse = ";"),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    )
  }
  if (ncol(mm) > MAX_GBM_MM_COLS_STOP || max_nlevels > MAX_GBM_FACTOR_LEVELS_STOP) {
    stop(
      "GBM preflight stop: context=", context_id,
      "; ncol_model_matrix=", ncol(mm),
      "; max_nlevels_factor=", max_nlevels,
      ". Consider collapsing factor levels or removing high-cardinality predictors."
    )
  }
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: print a small preview with an optional title during debugging.
print_head <- function(df, n = 10, title = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(title)) message(title)
  if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0L) {
    message("No rows to display.")
    return(invisible(FALSE))
  }
  print(utils::head(df, n))
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: assert that an object is data-frame-like before downstream code tries
# to treat it as tabular data.
assert_is_df <- function(x, context = "") {
  if (is.null(x)) {
    stop(context, ": object is NULL")
  }
  if (!inherits(x, "data.frame")) {
    stop(context, ": expected data.frame/tibble; got class=",
         paste(class(x), collapse = ", "), " typeof=", typeof(x))
  }
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: stop with a readable message when required columns are missing.
assert_has_cols <- function(df, cols, context = "") {
  missing <- setdiff(cols, names(df))
  if (length(missing)) {
    stop(context, ": missing required columns: ", paste(missing, collapse = ", "))
  }
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: obtain `nrow()` only after validating that the input is truly tabular.
safe_nrow <- function(df, context = "") {
  assert_is_df(df, context)
  nr <- nrow(df)
  if (!is.numeric(nr) || length(nr) != 1L || is.na(nr)) {
    stop(context, ": nrow(df) returned invalid value: ",
         paste0(capture.output(str(nr)), collapse = " "))
  }
  as.integer(nr)
}

# helper: expose the cumulative plot-drop log for debugging and export.
plot_drop_log <- function() {
  plot_drop_log_df
}

# helper: record rows dropped during figure preparation so suspicious plotting
# losses are always audit-able after a render.
log_plot_drop <- function(plot_name, reason, n_dropped,
                          n_before = NA_integer_, n_after = NA_integer_,
                          stage = NA_character_, extra = NA_character_) {
  plot_drop_log_df <<- dplyr::bind_rows(
    plot_drop_log(),
    data.frame(plot = plot_name, reason = reason, n_dropped = n_dropped,
               n_before = n_before, n_after = n_after,
               stage = stage, extra = extra,
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  )
  if (identical(PLOT_DROP_POLICY, "stop")) {
    stop("Plot data dropped in ", plot_name, ": ", reason)
  } else {
    warning("Plot data dropped in ", plot_name, ": ", reason, call. = FALSE)
  }
}

# helper: wrap model fitting so warnings, convergence flags, and simple
# separation diagnostics are captured in one standardized object.
fit_with_diagnostics <- function(fit_fun, context, prob_eps = PROB_EPS) {
  stopifnot(all(c("stage", "component", "analysis_variant", "model_type",
                  "group", "outcome", "imputation", "batch") %in% names(context)))
  cap <- capture_warnings(
    tryCatch(fit_fun(), error = function(e) e),
    context = context
  )
  append_warnings(cap$warnings)
  fit <- cap$value
  warn_msgs <- if (is.data.frame(cap$warnings)) cap$warnings$message else character()
  warning_n <- if (is.data.frame(cap$warnings)) nrow(cap$warnings) else 0L
  top_warning <- if (warning_n) warn_msgs[1] else NA_character_

  if (inherits(fit, "error")) {
    diag <- data.frame(
      stage = as.character(context$stage),
      component = as.character(context$component),
      analysis_variant = as.character(context$analysis_variant),
      model_type = as.character(context$model_type),
      group = as.character(context$group),
      outcome = as.character(context$outcome),
      imputation = as.integer(context$imputation),
      n_used = NA_integer_, events = NA_integer_,
      converged = NA, iter = NA_integer_,
      sep_flag = NA, nonconv_flag = NA,
      phat_low_flag = NA, phat_high_flag = NA,
      sep_warn_flag = FALSE,
      central_plot_instability_flag = FALSE,
      min_phat = NA_real_, max_phat = NA_real_,
      warning_n = warning_n, top_warning = top_warning,
      error_message = conditionMessage(fit),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    return(list(fit = NULL, diag = diag, warnings = cap$warnings))
  }

  phat <- tryCatch(fitted(fit), error = function(e) NA_real_)
  min_phat <- if (all(is.na(phat))) NA_real_ else min(phat, na.rm = TRUE)
  max_phat <- if (all(is.na(phat))) NA_real_ else max(phat, na.rm = TRUE)
  phat_low_flag <- is.finite(min_phat) && min_phat < prob_eps
  phat_high_flag <- is.finite(max_phat) && max_phat > 1 - prob_eps
  sep_warn_flag <- any(grepl("fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1", warn_msgs, fixed = TRUE)) ||
    any(grepl("separat", warn_msgs, ignore.case = TRUE))
  central_plot_instability_flag <- FALSE
  sep_flag <- phat_low_flag || phat_high_flag || sep_warn_flag

  conv_val <- if (!is.null(fit$converged)) isTRUE(fit$converged) else NA
  iter_val <- if (!is.null(fit$iter)) fit$iter else NA_integer_
  nonconv_flag <- isFALSE(conv_val) || any(grepl("did not converge", warn_msgs, fixed = TRUE))

  mf <- tryCatch(model.frame(fit), error = function(e) NULL)
  y <- if (!is.null(mf)) tryCatch(model.response(mf), error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
  n_used <- if (!is.null(y)) length(y) else NA_integer_
  events <- if (!is.null(y) && is.numeric(y)) sum(y == 1, na.rm = TRUE) else NA_integer_

  diag <- data.frame(
    stage = as.character(context$stage),
    component = as.character(context$component),
    analysis_variant = as.character(context$analysis_variant),
    model_type = as.character(context$model_type),
    group = as.character(context$group),
    outcome = as.character(context$outcome),
    imputation = as.integer(context$imputation),
    n_used = n_used, events = events,
    converged = conv_val, iter = iter_val,
    sep_flag = sep_flag, nonconv_flag = nonconv_flag,
    phat_low_flag = phat_low_flag,
    phat_high_flag = phat_high_flag,
    sep_warn_flag = sep_warn_flag,
    central_plot_instability_flag = central_plot_instability_flag,
    min_phat = min_phat, max_phat = max_phat,
    warning_n = warning_n, top_warning = top_warning,
    error_message = NA_character_,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  list(fit = fit, diag = diag, warnings = cap$warnings)
}

# helper: split broad separation/extreme-probability diagnostics into
# manuscript-critical failures versus tail-only warnings.
enrich_model_diagnostic_flags <- function(diag_df, prob_eps = PROB_EPS) {
  if (!is.data.frame(diag_df) || !nrow(diag_df)) return(diag_df)
  if (!"top_warning" %in% names(diag_df)) diag_df$top_warning <- NA_character_
  if (!"min_phat" %in% names(diag_df)) diag_df$min_phat <- NA_real_
  if (!"max_phat" %in% names(diag_df)) diag_df$max_phat <- NA_real_
  if (!"converged" %in% names(diag_df)) diag_df$converged <- NA
  if (!"nonconv_flag" %in% names(diag_df)) diag_df$nonconv_flag <- FALSE
  if (!"sep_flag" %in% names(diag_df)) diag_df$sep_flag <- FALSE
  if (!"phat_low_flag" %in% names(diag_df)) diag_df$phat_low_flag <- NA
  if (!"phat_high_flag" %in% names(diag_df)) diag_df$phat_high_flag <- NA
  if (!"sep_warn_flag" %in% names(diag_df)) diag_df$sep_warn_flag <- NA
  if (!"central_plot_instability_flag" %in% names(diag_df)) {
    diag_df$central_plot_instability_flag <- NA
  }
  warn_chr <- as.character(diag_df$top_warning)
  warn_chr[is.na(warn_chr)] <- ""
  diag_df$phat_low_flag <- dplyr::coalesce(as.logical(diag_df$phat_low_flag), FALSE) |
    (is.finite(diag_df$min_phat) & diag_df$min_phat < prob_eps)
  diag_df$phat_high_flag <- dplyr::coalesce(as.logical(diag_df$phat_high_flag), FALSE) |
    (is.finite(diag_df$max_phat) & diag_df$max_phat > 1 - prob_eps)
  diag_df$sep_warn_flag <- dplyr::coalesce(as.logical(diag_df$sep_warn_flag), FALSE) |
    grepl("fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1", warn_chr, fixed = TRUE) |
    grepl("separat", warn_chr, ignore.case = TRUE)
  diag_df$nonconv_flag <- dplyr::coalesce(as.logical(diag_df$nonconv_flag), FALSE) |
    dplyr::coalesce(!as.logical(diag_df$converged), FALSE) |
    grepl("did not converge", warn_chr, fixed = TRUE)
  diag_df$central_plot_instability_flag <- dplyr::coalesce(
    as.logical(diag_df$central_plot_instability_flag),
    FALSE
  )
  diag_df$sep_flag <- dplyr::coalesce(as.logical(diag_df$sep_flag), FALSE) |
    diag_df$phat_low_flag | diag_df$phat_high_flag | diag_df$sep_warn_flag
  diag_df$diagnostic_class <- dplyr::case_when(
    diag_df$nonconv_flag | diag_df$sep_warn_flag | diag_df$central_plot_instability_flag ~ "fail",
    diag_df$phat_low_flag | diag_df$phat_high_flag | diag_df$sep_flag ~ "warn",
    TRUE ~ "pass"
  )
  diag_df$diagnostic_status <- dplyr::case_when(
    diag_df$diagnostic_class == "fail" ~ "FAIL",
    diag_df$diagnostic_class == "warn" ~ "PASS_WITH_WARNINGS",
    TRUE ~ "PASS"
  )
  diag_df
}

# helper: confirm that a pooled MI variance object is finite before it is used
# to generate manuscript-facing confidence intervals.
pooled_mi_vcov_check <- function(pooled) {
  if (is.null(pooled$variance)) stop("MIcombine object missing variance matrix.")
  V <- pooled$variance
  if (any(!is.finite(V))) stop("Non-finite pooled variance detected.")
  within <- attr(pooled, "within")
  between <- attr(pooled, "between")
  if (!is.null(within) && any(!is.finite(within))) stop("Non-finite within variance.")
  if (!is.null(between) && any(!is.finite(between))) stop("Non-finite between variance.")
  invisible(TRUE)
}

# helper: one project-wide publication theme so manuscript and supplement
# figures inherit the same typography and guide styling.
theme_pub <- function(base_size = 10, base_family = "", legend_position = "top") {
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = base_size, base_family = base_family) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0, size = ggplot2::rel(1.05)),
      plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(color = "grey20"),
      plot.caption = ggplot2::element_text(size = ggplot2::rel(0.85), color = "grey25", hjust = 0),
      axis.title = ggplot2::element_text(color = "grey10"),
      axis.text = ggplot2::element_text(color = "grey10"),
      panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.y = ggplot2::element_line(color = "grey78", linewidth = 0.35),
      axis.line = ggplot2::element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.35),
      axis.ticks = ggplot2::element_line(color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.35),
      legend.position = legend_position,
      legend.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.background = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      legend.key = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = "grey75", linewidth = 0.35),
      strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold")
    )
}

# helper: save a diagnostic plot with explicit dimensions and a vector PDF path
# when appropriate, avoiding IDE-dependent defaults.
save_diag_plot <- function(p, file, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300, bg = "white") {
  if (grepl("\\.pdf$", file, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    ggplot2::ggsave(
      filename = file,
      plot = p,
      width = width,
      height = height,
      units = units,
      device = grDevices::cairo_pdf,
      bg = bg
    )
    return(invisible(file))
  }
  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = file,
    plot = p,
    width = width,
    height = height,
    units = units,
    dpi = dpi,
    bg = bg
  )
  invisible(file)
}

plot_registry_path <- results_path("plot_registry.csv")

# helper: load the registry of previously written plots for this render family.
read_plot_registry <- function() {
  if (file.exists(plot_registry_path)) {
    utils::read.csv(plot_registry_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  } else {
    data.frame(
      run_id = character(), run_ts = character(),
      plot_name = character(), fig_path = character(), md5 = character(),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
}

# helper: persist the plot registry after each update.
write_plot_registry <- function(df) {
  write_csv_safely(df, plot_registry_path, row_names = FALSE)
}

# helper: add one saved plot file to the registry, or log a duplicate if a
# chunk tries to emit the same named figure twice.
register_plot_file <- function(plot_name, file, run_id = diag_run_id, run_ts = diag_run_ts) {
  md5 <- as.character(tools::md5sum(file))
  reg <- read_plot_registry()
  dup_name <- nrow(reg) > 0 &&
    any(reg$run_id == run_id & reg$plot_name == plot_name, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (dup_name) {
    log_plot_drop(
      plot_name,
      "duplicate_figure",
      n_dropped = 1,
      n_before = NA_integer_,
      n_after = NA_integer_,
      stage = "plot_registry",
      extra = file
    )
    return(FALSE)
  }
  reg <- dplyr::bind_rows(
    reg,
    data.frame(run_id = run_id, run_ts = run_ts,
               plot_name = plot_name, fig_path = file, md5 = md5,
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  )
  write_plot_registry(reg)
  TRUE
}

# helper: save a figure to disk and include it in the notebook only if that
# named figure has not already been emitted in the current render.
print_plot_once <- function(p, plot_name, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300) {
  file_png <- results_path("figs", paste0(plot_name, ".png"))
  file_pdf <- results_path("figs", paste0(plot_name, ".pdf"))
  file_preview <- results_path("figs", paste0(plot_name, "-report-preview.png"))
  save_diag_plot(p, file_png, width = width, height = height, dpi = dpi)
  save_diag_plot(p, file_pdf, width = width, height = height, dpi = dpi)
  # Use a lighter inline preview while keeping manuscript artifacts high resolution.
  save_diag_plot(p, file_preview, width = width, height = height, dpi = min(dpi, 96))
  if (register_plot_file(plot_name, file_png)) {
    if (isTRUE(VALIDATION_INLINE_ANALYSIS_PREVIEWS)) {
      mark_float_emitted()
      render_inline_graphic(file_preview)
    } else {
      invisible(file_preview)
    }
  } else {
    invisible(NULL)
  }
}

# helper: build the canonical on-disk path for any artifact stub/ext pair.
artifact_file_path <- function(stub, ext, subdir = NULL) {
  if (is.null(stub) || is.na(stub) || !nzchar(stub)) return(NA_character_)
  if (is.null(subdir) || is.na(subdir) || !nzchar(subdir)) {
    return(results_path(paste0(stub, ".", ext)))
  }
  results_path(subdir, paste0(stub, ".", ext))
}

# helper: copy one canonical artifact to one or more manuscript-facing alias
# names without changing the original analysis-oriented filename.
write_artifact_alias <- function(source_stub, alias_stub, exts,
                                 source_subdir = NULL,
                                 alias_subdir = source_subdir) {
  copied <- character(0)
  missing <- character(0)

  for (ext in exts) {
    src <- artifact_file_path(source_stub, ext, source_subdir)
    dst <- artifact_file_path(alias_stub, ext, alias_subdir)
    if (!file.exists(src)) {
      missing <- c(missing, src)
      next
    }
    if (fs::path_norm(src) != fs::path_norm(dst)) {
      fs::file_copy(src, dst, overwrite = TRUE)
    }
    copied <- c(copied, dst)
  }

  if (length(missing)) {
    stop(
      "Missing source artifact(s) for alias [",
      alias_stub,
      "]: ",
      paste(vapply(missing, pretty_path, character(1L)), collapse = ", ")
    )
  }
  invisible(copied)
}

# helper: mirror one saved figure into its manuscript alias path.
write_plot_alias <- function(plot_name, alias_name,
                             source_subdir = "figs",
                             alias_subdir = source_subdir) {
  write_artifact_alias(
    source_stub = plot_name,
    alias_stub = alias_name,
    exts = c("png", "pdf"),
    source_subdir = source_subdir,
    alias_subdir = alias_subdir
  )
}

# helper: mirror one saved table export into its manuscript alias path.
write_table_alias <- function(file_stub, alias_stub,
                              source_subdir = NULL,
                              alias_subdir = source_subdir) {
  write_artifact_alias(
    source_stub = file_stub,
    alias_stub = alias_stub,
    exts = c("csv", "pdf"),
    source_subdir = source_subdir,
    alias_subdir = alias_subdir
  )
}

# helper: force a figure to appear in the notebook even if a previous chunk
# already emitted the same named artifact.
print_plot_force <- function(p, plot_name, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300) {
  # Use this for key publication figures that must always appear in the PDF.
  file_png <- results_path("figs", paste0(plot_name, ".png"))
  file_pdf <- results_path("figs", paste0(plot_name, ".pdf"))
  file_preview <- results_path("figs", paste0(plot_name, "-report-preview.png"))
  save_diag_plot(p, file_png, width = width, height = height, dpi = dpi)
  save_diag_plot(p, file_pdf, width = width, height = height, dpi = dpi)
  save_diag_plot(p, file_preview, width = width, height = height, dpi = min(dpi, 96))
  register_plot_file(plot_name, file_png)
  if (isTRUE(VALIDATION_INLINE_ANALYSIS_PREVIEWS)) {
    mark_float_emitted()
    render_inline_graphic(file_preview)
  } else {
    invisible(file_preview)
  }
}

poster_export_status_path <- results_path("poster_figure_export_status.csv")
poster_svg_package <- paste0("svg", "lite")

# helper: reset the lightweight poster export audit for the current render.
initialize_poster_export_status <- function(path = poster_export_status_path) {
  empty_status <- data.frame(
    run_id = character(),
    run_ts = character(),
    file_stub = character(),
    format = character(),
    path = character(),
    width_in = numeric(),
    height_in = numeric(),
    dpi = integer(),
    expected_width_px = integer(),
    expected_height_px = integer(),
    exists = logical(),
    size_bytes = numeric(),
    status = character(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  write_csv_safely(empty_status, path, row_names = FALSE)
  invisible(path)
}

# helper: fail clearly when a poster export did not create a real output file.
assert_poster_output_file <- function(path, context = "poster export") {
  if (is.null(path) || is.na(path) || !nzchar(path) || !file.exists(path)) {
    stop(context, ": missing output file: ", path)
  }
  file_size <- unname(file.info(path)$size)
  if (!is.finite(file_size) || file_size <= 0) {
    stop(context, ": output file is empty: ", pretty_path(path))
  }
  invisible(path)
}

# helper: append one row to the poster export audit without retaining state in
# memory across hidden poster chunks.
record_poster_export_status <- function(file_stub, format, path,
                                        width_in, height_in, dpi,
                                        status = "created") {
  file_exists <- !is.na(path) && nzchar(path) && file.exists(path)
  file_size <- if (file_exists) unname(file.info(path)$size) else NA_real_
  status_row <- data.frame(
    run_id = diag_run_id,
    run_ts = diag_run_ts,
    file_stub = file_stub,
    format = format,
    path = if (is.na(path)) NA_character_ else display_path(path, must_exist = FALSE),
    width_in = width_in,
    height_in = height_in,
    dpi = as.integer(dpi),
    expected_width_px = if (identical(format, "png")) as.integer(ceiling(width_in * dpi)) else NA_integer_,
    expected_height_px = if (identical(format, "png")) as.integer(ceiling(height_in * dpi)) else NA_integer_,
    exists = file_exists,
    size_bytes = file_size,
    status = status,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  existing_status <- if (file.exists(poster_export_status_path)) {
    utils::read.csv(poster_export_status_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  } else {
    status_row[0, , drop = FALSE]
  }
  if (!nrow(existing_status)) {
    existing_status <- status_row[0, , drop = FALSE]
  }
  write_csv_safely(
    dplyr::bind_rows(existing_status, status_row),
    poster_export_status_path,
    row_names = FALSE
  )
  invisible(status_row)
}

# helper: optional SVG device lookup. Keeping the package name dynamic prevents
# dependency audits from treating SVG support as a required direct dependency.
poster_svg_available <- function() {
  isTRUE(do.call("requireNamespace", list(package = poster_svg_package, quietly = TRUE)))
}

# helper: open an SVG device when svglite is installed, preserving requested
# text sizing behavior on svglite versions that expose fix_text_size.
open_poster_svg_device <- function(filename, width, height, bg = "white") {
  svg_fun <- getExportedValue(poster_svg_package, "svglite")
  svg_args <- list(
    file = filename,
    width = width,
    height = height,
    bg = bg
  )
  if ("fix_text_size" %in% names(formals(svg_fun))) {
    svg_args$fix_text_size <- FALSE
  }
  do.call(svg_fun, svg_args)
}

# helper: save poster-sized ggplot outputs at their final display dimensions,
# with high-resolution PNG plus vector PDF/SVG paths when devices are present.
save_poster_ggplot_outputs <- function(plot, file_stub, width_in, height_in,
                                       dpi = POSTER_DPI,
                                       output_dir = results_path("figs"),
                                       bg = "white",
                                       register_png = TRUE) {
  if (!inherits(plot, "ggplot")) {
    stop("save_poster_ggplot_outputs() requires a ggplot object.")
  }
  fs::dir_create(output_dir, recurse = TRUE)
  png_file <- fs::path(output_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".png"))
  pdf_file <- fs::path(output_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".pdf"))
  svg_file <- fs::path(output_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".svg"))

  png_device <- function(filename, width, height, units, res = dpi, ...) {
    dots <- list(...)
    if (!is.null(dots$dpi)) res <- dots$dpi
    ragg::agg_png(
      filename = filename,
      width = width,
      height = height,
      units = units,
      res = res,
      background = bg
    )
  }
  pdf_device <- if (capabilities("cairo")) {
    function(filename, width, height, ...) {
      grDevices::cairo_pdf(filename = filename, width = width, height = height, bg = bg)
    }
  } else {
    function(filename, width, height, ...) {
      grDevices::pdf(file = filename, width = width, height = height, bg = bg)
    }
  }
  svg_device <- function(filename, width, height, ...) {
    open_poster_svg_device(filename = filename, width = width, height = height, bg = bg)
  }

  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = png_file,
    plot = plot,
    width = width_in,
    height = height_in,
    units = "in",
    dpi = dpi,
    device = png_device,
    bg = bg,
    limitsize = FALSE
  )
  assert_poster_output_file(png_file, context = paste(file_stub, "PNG export"))
  record_poster_export_status(file_stub, "png", png_file, width_in, height_in, dpi)

  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = pdf_file,
    plot = plot,
    width = width_in,
    height = height_in,
    units = "in",
    device = pdf_device,
    bg = bg,
    limitsize = FALSE
  )
  assert_poster_output_file(pdf_file, context = paste(file_stub, "PDF export"))
  record_poster_export_status(file_stub, "pdf", pdf_file, width_in, height_in, dpi)

  if (poster_svg_available()) {
    ggplot2::ggsave(
      filename = svg_file,
      plot = plot,
      width = width_in,
      height = height_in,
      units = "in",
      device = svg_device,
      bg = bg,
      limitsize = FALSE
    )
    assert_poster_output_file(svg_file, context = paste(file_stub, "SVG export"))
    record_poster_export_status(file_stub, "svg", svg_file, width_in, height_in, dpi)
  } else {
    svg_file <- NA_character_
    record_poster_export_status(
      file_stub,
      "svg",
      svg_file,
      width_in,
      height_in,
      dpi,
      status = "skipped_svglite_unavailable"
    )
  }

  if (isTRUE(register_png)) {
    register_plot_file(file_stub, png_file)
  }
  invisible(list(png = png_file, pdf = pdf_file, svg = svg_file))
}

# helper: parallel poster export helper for grid/grob objects, kept here for
# future poster figures that are not native ggplot objects.
save_poster_grob_outputs <- function(grob, file_stub, width_in, height_in,
                                     dpi = POSTER_DPI,
                                     output_dir = results_path("figs"),
                                     bg = "white",
                                     register_png = TRUE) {
  if (!inherits(grob, "grob") && !inherits(grob, "gTree")) {
    stop("save_poster_grob_outputs() requires a grid grob or gTree.")
  }
  fs::dir_create(output_dir, recurse = TRUE)
  png_file <- fs::path(output_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".png"))
  pdf_file <- fs::path(output_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".pdf"))
  svg_file <- fs::path(output_dir, paste0(file_stub, ".svg"))
  draw_grob <- function(new_page = TRUE) {
    if (isTRUE(new_page)) grid::grid.newpage()
    grid::grid.draw(grob)
  }

  ragg::agg_png(
    filename = png_file,
    width = width_in,
    height = height_in,
    units = "in",
    res = dpi,
    background = bg
  )
  draw_grob(new_page = TRUE)
  grDevices::dev.off()
  assert_poster_output_file(png_file, context = paste(file_stub, "PNG export"))
  record_poster_export_status(file_stub, "png", png_file, width_in, height_in, dpi)

  if (capabilities("cairo")) {
    grDevices::cairo_pdf(filename = pdf_file, width = width_in, height = height_in, bg = bg)
  } else {
    grDevices::pdf(file = pdf_file, width = width_in, height = height_in, bg = bg)
  }
  draw_grob(new_page = FALSE)
  grDevices::dev.off()
  assert_poster_output_file(pdf_file, context = paste(file_stub, "PDF export"))
  record_poster_export_status(file_stub, "pdf", pdf_file, width_in, height_in, dpi)

  if (poster_svg_available()) {
    open_poster_svg_device(
      filename = svg_file,
      width = width_in,
      height = height_in,
      bg = bg
    )
    draw_grob(new_page = FALSE)
    grDevices::dev.off()
    assert_poster_output_file(svg_file, context = paste(file_stub, "SVG export"))
    record_poster_export_status(file_stub, "svg", svg_file, width_in, height_in, dpi)
  } else {
    svg_file <- NA_character_
    record_poster_export_status(
      file_stub,
      "svg",
      svg_file,
      width_in,
      height_in,
      dpi,
      status = "skipped_svglite_unavailable"
    )
  }

  if (isTRUE(register_png)) {
    register_plot_file(file_stub, png_file)
  }
  invisible(list(png = png_file, pdf = pdf_file, svg = svg_file))
}

initialize_poster_export_status()

# helper: map a model-matrix term back to its parent feature so SHAP fallbacks
# and coefficient-based summaries stay interpretable.
term_to_parent_feature <- function(term, feature_names) {
  if (is.na(term) || identical(term, "(Intercept)")) return(NA_character_)
  clean_term <- gsub("`", "", term, fixed = TRUE)
  feature_names <- sort(unique(feature_names), decreasing = TRUE)
  hit <- feature_names[vapply(feature_names, function(v) grepl(v, clean_term, fixed = TRUE), logical(1))]
  if (length(hit)) return(hit[[1]])
  NA_character_
}

covariate_display_labels <- c(
  age_at_encounter = "Age",
  curr_bmi = "BMI",
  temp_new = "Temperature",
  sbp = "Systolic blood pressure",
  dbp = "Diastolic blood pressure",
  hr = "Heart rate",
  sodium = "Sodium",
  serum_cr = "Serum creatinine",
  serum_hco3 = "Serum bicarbonate",
  serum_cl = "Serum chloride",
  serum_lac = "Serum lactate",
  serum_k = "Serum potassium",
  wbc = "White blood cell count",
  plt = "Platelet count",
  serum_phos = "Serum phosphate",
  serum_ca = "Serum calcium",
  sex = "Sex",
  race_ethnicity = "Race/ethnicity",
  location = "Region",
  encounter_type = "Encounter type",
  copd = "COPD",
  asthma = "Asthma",
  osa = "OSA",
  chf = "CHF",
  acute_nmd = "Neuromuscular disease",
  phtn = "Pulmonary hypertension",
  ckd = "Chronic kidney disease",
  dm = "Diabetes"
)

binary_covariate_labels <- c("copd", "asthma", "osa", "chf", "acute_nmd", "phtn", "ckd", "dm")

categorical_level_labels <- list(
  sex = c("0" = "Female", "1" = "Male", "2" = "Unknown/other", Male = "Male", Female = "Female"),
  race_ethnicity = c(
    "0" = "White", "1" = "Black", "2" = "Hispanic", "3" = "Asian",
    "4" = "AI/AN", "5" = "NH/PI", "6" = "Unknown"
  ),
  location = c("0" = "South", "1" = "Northeast", "2" = "Midwest", "3" = "West"),
  encounter_type = c(
    "2" = "Emergency", "3" = "Inpatient",
    Emergency = "Emergency", Inpatient = "Inpatient"
  )
)

pretty_covariate_value <- function(feature, value) {
  value <- trimws(as.character(value))
  if (identical(value, "<NA>")) return("missing indicator")
  if (feature %in% binary_covariate_labels && value %in% c("0", "1")) {
    return(ifelse(value == "1", "yes", "no"))
  }
  level_map <- categorical_level_labels[[feature]]
  if (!is.null(level_map) && value %in% names(level_map)) return(unname(level_map[[value]]))
  gsub("_", " ", value, fixed = TRUE)
}

display_covariate_label_one <- function(term) {
  term <- as.character(term)
  if (!nzchar(term) || is.na(term)) return(term)
  if (identical(term, "Overall (mean |SMD|)")) return(term)

  base_names <- sort(names(covariate_display_labels), decreasing = TRUE)
  for (feature in base_names) {
    feature_label <- unname(covariate_display_labels[[feature]])
    if (identical(term, feature)) return(feature_label)

    prefix_colon <- paste0(feature, ":")
    if (startsWith(term, prefix_colon)) {
      value <- substring(term, nchar(prefix_colon) + 1L)
      return(paste0(feature_label, ": ", pretty_covariate_value(feature, value)))
    }

    prefix_us <- paste0(feature, "_")
    if (startsWith(term, prefix_us)) {
      value <- substring(term, nchar(prefix_us) + 1L)
      return(paste0(feature_label, ": ", pretty_covariate_value(feature, value)))
    }

    if (startsWith(term, feature) && nchar(term) > nchar(feature)) {
      value <- substring(term, nchar(feature) + 1L)
      if (feature %in% names(categorical_level_labels) && nzchar(value)) {
        return(paste0(feature_label, ": ", pretty_covariate_value(feature, value)))
      }
    }
  }

  tools::toTitleCase(gsub("_", " ", term, fixed = TRUE))
}

display_covariate_label <- function(term) {
  vapply(term, display_covariate_label_one, character(1L), USE.NAMES = FALSE)
}

# helper: subsample rows reproducibly before SHAP-style calculations so
# diagnostics remain stable while keeping runtime bounded.
sample_rows_for_shap <- function(df, n_max = SHAP_SAMPLE_N, seed = 20251206L) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df) || is.matrix(df))
  if (nrow(df) <= n_max) return(df)
  set.seed(seed)
  df[sample.int(nrow(df), size = n_max, replace = FALSE), , drop = FALSE]
}

# helper: refit the GBM weighting model with the exact options needed for the
# downstream feature-contribution diagnostics.
fit_weightit_gbm_for_shap <- function(formula_obj, data_obj) {
  args <- c(
    list(
      formula = formula_obj,
      data = data_obj,
      method = "gbm",
      estimand = "ATE",
      missing = "ind",
      include.obj = TRUE
    ),
    gbm_params
  )
  do.call(WeightIt::weightit, args)
}

# helper: compute the top GBM contribution features for one non-MI weighting
# model, with a deterministic fallback to relative influence if `fastshap` fails.
extract_nonmi_gbm_shap_top <- function(weight_obj, x_df, feature_names, group_label,
                                       nsim = SHAP_NSIM, top_n = SHAP_TOP_N) {
  if (!HAS_FASTSHAP) {
    return(
      data.frame(
        group = character(),
        feature = character(),
        mean_abs_shap = numeric(),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    )
  }
  stopifnot(inherits(weight_obj, "weightit"))
  stopifnot(!is.null(weight_obj$obj))
  gbm_obj <- weight_obj$obj
  best_tree <- weight_obj$info$best.tree
  if (is.null(best_tree) || !is.finite(best_tree) || best_tree <= 0) {
    best_tree <- gbm_obj$n.trees
  }
  x_use <- x_df[, feature_names, drop = FALSE]
  char_cols <- names(x_use)[vapply(x_use, is.character, logical(1))]
  if (length(char_cols)) {
    for (nm in char_cols) x_use[[nm]] <- factor(x_use[[nm]])
  }
  x_use <- droplevels_all(x_use)
  x_background <- as.data.frame(
    sample_rows_for_shap(x_use, n_max = max(SHAP_SAMPLE_N, 5000L), seed = 20251206L)
  )
  x_shap <- as.data.frame(
    sample_rows_for_shap(x_use, n_max = SHAP_SAMPLE_N, seed = 20251206L)
  )

  pred_fun <- function(object, newdata) {
    new_use <- newdata[, object$var.names, drop = FALSE]
    as.numeric(stats::predict(object, newdata = new_use, n.trees = best_tree, type = "link"))
  }

  shap_mat <- tryCatch(
    fastshap::explain(
      object = gbm_obj,
      feature_names = feature_names,
      X = x_background,
      newdata = x_shap,
      pred_wrapper = pred_fun,
      nsim = nsim,
      adjust = FALSE
    ),
    error = function(e) {
      warning(
        "fastshap failed for ", group_label,
        "; falling back to GBM relative influence. ",
        conditionMessage(e),
        call. = FALSE
      )
      NULL
    }
  )

  if (is.null(shap_mat)) {
    gbm_imp <- tryCatch(
      gbm::summary.gbm(gbm_obj, plotit = FALSE),
      error = function(e) data.frame(var = character(), rel.inf = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    )
    if (!nrow(gbm_imp)) {
      return(data.frame(group = character(), feature = character(), mean_abs_shap = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
    }
    return(
      gbm_imp |>
        dplyr::transmute(
          group = group_label,
          feature = as.character(var),
          mean_abs_shap = as.numeric(rel.inf) / 100
        ) |>
        dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(mean_abs_shap)) |>
        dplyr::slice_head(n = top_n)
    )
  }

  out <- data.frame(
    group = group_label,
    feature = colnames(shap_mat),
    mean_abs_shap = colMeans(abs(shap_mat), na.rm = TRUE),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ) |>
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(mean_abs_shap)) |>
    dplyr::slice_head(n = top_n)
  out
}

# helper: plot the top contribution features side by side for ABG and VBG so
# readers can compare the ordering models directly.
plot_shap_top10_two_panel <- function(df_top, title_text, x_label) {
  # Keep only top-N per cohort and force descending order inside each panel.
  df_top_plot <- df_top |>
    dplyr::group_by(group) |>
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(mean_abs_shap), .by_group = TRUE) |>
    dplyr::slice_head(n = SHAP_TOP_N) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      feature_rank = dplyr::row_number(),
      feature_plot = paste0(sprintf("%02d", feature_rank), ". ", display_covariate_label(feature)),
      group = factor(group, levels = c("ABG", "VBG"))
    ) |>
    dplyr::ungroup()

  # Prefixing with rank makes ordering deterministic in each facet.
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    df_top_plot,
    ggplot2::aes(
      x = mean_abs_shap,
      y = stats::reorder(feature_plot, mean_abs_shap),
      fill = group
    )
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~group, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = stringr::str_wrap(title_text, width = 65),
      x = x_label,
      y = NULL
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) sub("^[0-9]+\\.\\s*", "", x)) +
    theme_pub(base_size = 10)
}

# helper: prepend a whole-cohort balance summary row so the love plot reports
# both term-level and overall balance shifts.
add_overall_love_rows <- function(df_in, overall_label = "Overall (mean |SMD|)") {
  # Summarize whole-plot balance for each cohort/sample pair and display it
  # as the top row so readers can quickly judge global balance shift.
  overall <- df_in |>
    dplyr::group_by(group, sample) |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      abs_smd = mean(abs_smd, na.rm = TRUE),
      abs_smd_raw = mean(abs_smd_raw, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(term = overall_label)
  dplyr::bind_rows(overall, df_in)
}

select_loveplot_display_terms <- function(df_in, top_n = 20L,
                                          overall_label = "Overall (mean |SMD|)") {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df_in), top_n > 0)
  top_terms <- df_in |>
    dplyr::filter(.data$term != overall_label) |>
    dplyr::group_by(.data$group, .data$term) |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      display_score = max(c(.data$abs_smd, .data$abs_smd_raw), na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    dplyr::arrange(.data$group, dplyr::desc(.data$display_score), .data$term) |>
    dplyr::group_by(.data$group) |>
    dplyr::slice_head(n = top_n) |>
    dplyr::ungroup() |>
    dplyr::select(.data$group, .data$term)

  dplyr::bind_rows(
    df_in |> dplyr::filter(.data$term == overall_label),
    df_in |> dplyr::semi_join(top_terms, by = c("group", "term"))
  )
}

# helper: apply one consistent love-plot layout across weighting diagnostics.
loveplot_style <- function(df_in, title_text, label_width = NULL,
                           base_size = MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_SPARSE_BASE_SIZE,
                           segment_linewidth = MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_SEGMENT_LINEWIDTH,
                           reference_linewidth = MANUSCRIPT_REFERENCE_LINEWIDTH,
                           point_size_values = MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_POINT_SIZE) {
  # Build a classic love-plot view with:
  # 1) a segment from Raw -> IPW for each term, and
  # 2) explicit Raw/IPW points so both samples are always visible.
  # This avoids overplotting where one sample can hide the other.
  df_plot <- df_in |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      sample = factor(sample, levels = c("Raw", "IPW")),
      term_label = display_covariate_label(term),
      term_label = if (is.null(label_width)) term_label else stringr::str_wrap(term_label, width = label_width),
      order_val = dplyr::if_else(
        term == "Overall (mean |SMD|)",
        max(abs_smd_raw, na.rm = TRUE) + 1,
        abs_smd_raw
      )
    )

  df_wide <- df_plot |>
    dplyr::select(group, term, term_label, order_val, sample, abs_smd) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = sample, values_from = abs_smd)

  ggplot2::ggplot(df_plot, ggplot2::aes(y = stats::reorder(term_label, order_val))) +
    ggplot2::geom_segment(
      data = df_wide,
      ggplot2::aes(
        x = Raw,
        xend = IPW,
        y = stats::reorder(term_label, order_val),
        yend = stats::reorder(term_label, order_val)
      ),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "grey45",
      linewidth = segment_linewidth,
      alpha = 0.9
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(
      data = df_plot |> dplyr::filter(sample == "Raw"),
      ggplot2::aes(x = abs_smd, color = sample, shape = sample, size = term == "Overall (mean |SMD|)"),
      alpha = 0.95
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(
      data = df_plot |> dplyr::filter(sample == "IPW"),
      ggplot2::aes(x = abs_smd, color = sample, shape = sample, size = term == "Overall (mean |SMD|)"),
      alpha = 0.95
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(
      xintercept = 0.10,
      linetype = "dashed",
      color = "grey20",
      linewidth = reference_linewidth
    ) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~group, ncol = 2) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = c("Raw" = "#F8766D", "IPW" = "#00BFC4")) +
    ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(values = c("Raw" = 16, "IPW" = 17), guide = "none") +
    ggplot2::scale_size_manual(values = point_size_values, guide = "none") +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = stringr::str_wrap(title_text, width = 65),
      x = "Absolute Standardized Mean Differences",
      y = NULL,
      color = "Sample"
    ) +
    theme_pub(base_size = base_size) +
    ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "top")
}

CO2_RATE_OUTCOMES <- c("imv_proc", "niv_proc", "death_60d", "hypercap_resp_failure")
CO2_RATE_OUTCOME_LABELS <- c(
  imv_proc = "IMV",
  niv_proc = "NIV",
  death_60d = "Death (60d)",
  hypercap_resp_failure = "Hypercapnic RF"
)

# helper: bin CO2 values and compute observed outcome rates for exploratory
# shape checks before the fully modeled spline figures are built.
compute_co2_bin_rates <- function(df, co2_var, outcomes, outcome_labels,
                                  weight_var = NULL, q = c(0.02, 0.98),
                                  bin_width = 1, cohort_label = NA_character_) {
  assert_is_df(df, context = paste0("compute_co2_bin_rates(", co2_var, ")"))
  stopifnot(co2_var %in% names(df))
  stopifnot(all(outcomes %in% names(df)))
  stopifnot(length(q) == 2L, q[1] < q[2], is.finite(bin_width), bin_width > 0)
  if (!is.null(weight_var)) stopifnot(weight_var %in% names(df))

  co2_vals <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[co2_var]]))
  keep <- is.finite(co2_vals)
  if (!any(keep)) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      cohort = character(), outcome = character(), outcome_label = character(),
      bin_lo = numeric(), bin_mid = numeric(), n_bin = integer(), rate = numeric()
    ))
  }

  q_lohi <- stats::quantile(co2_vals[keep], probs = q, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)
  keep <- keep & co2_vals >= q_lohi[1] & co2_vals <= q_lohi[2]
  if (!is.null(weight_var)) {
    w <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[weight_var]]))
    keep <- keep & is.finite(w) & !is.na(w) & (w > 0)
  }
  if (!any(keep)) {
    return(tibble::tibble(
      cohort = character(), outcome = character(), outcome_label = character(),
      bin_lo = numeric(), bin_mid = numeric(), n_bin = integer(), rate = numeric()
    ))
  }

  d <- df[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  d$co2_work <- co2_vals[keep]
  d$bin_lo <- floor(d$co2_work / bin_width) * bin_width
  d$bin_mid <- d$bin_lo + (bin_width / 2)
  if (!is.null(weight_var)) {
    d$w_work <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(d[[weight_var]]))
  }

  rate_rows <- lapply(outcomes, function(outcome_nm) {
    y <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(d[[outcome_nm]] == 1))
    y[is.na(d[[outcome_nm]])] <- NA_real_
    tmp <- d[, c("bin_lo", "bin_mid"), drop = FALSE]
    tmp$y <- y
    if (!is.null(weight_var)) tmp$w <- d$w_work
    if (!is.null(weight_var)) {
      tmp <- tmp[is.finite(tmp$w) & !is.na(tmp$w) & !is.na(tmp$y), , drop = FALSE]
    } else {
      tmp <- tmp[!is.na(tmp$y), , drop = FALSE]
    }
    if (!nrow(tmp)) return(NULL)

    if (!is.null(weight_var)) {
      out <- tmp |>
        dplyr::group_by(bin_lo, bin_mid) |>
        dplyr::summarise(
          n_bin = dplyr::n(),
          rate = ifelse(sum(w, na.rm = TRUE) > 0, sum(y * w, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(w, na.rm = TRUE), NA_real_),
          .groups = "drop"
        )
    } else {
      out <- tmp |>
        dplyr::group_by(bin_lo, bin_mid) |>
        dplyr::summarise(
          n_bin = dplyr::n(),
          rate = mean(y, na.rm = TRUE),
          .groups = "drop"
        )
    }

    out |>
      dplyr::mutate(
        cohort = cohort_label,
        outcome = outcome_nm,
        outcome_label = dplyr::if_else(
          outcome_nm %in% names(outcome_labels),
          as.character(outcome_labels[[outcome_nm]]),
          outcome_nm
        )
      ) |>
      dplyr::select(cohort, outcome, outcome_label, bin_lo, bin_mid, n_bin, rate)
  })

  dplyr::bind_rows(rate_rows)
}

# helper: average binned outcome-rate diagnostics across imputations for a
# quick MI-aware visual check of the empirical shape.
pool_mi_bin_rates <- function(rate_df_all_imps) {
  assert_is_df(rate_df_all_imps, context = "pool_mi_bin_rates")
  stopifnot("imp" %in% names(rate_df_all_imps))
  rate_df_all_imps |>
    dplyr::group_by(cohort, outcome, outcome_label, bin_lo, bin_mid) |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      n_bin = mean(n_bin, na.rm = TRUE),
      rate = mean(rate, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_imp_contrib = sum(is.finite(rate)),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

# helper: render the exploratory binned-rate panels with shared styling and an
# explicit reference line at the chosen CO2 anchor.
plot_co2_bin_rate_panel <- function(rate_df, title, x_label, vline_at) {
  assert_is_df(rate_df, context = "plot_co2_bin_rate_panel")
  stopifnot(all(c("bin_mid", "n_bin", "rate", "outcome_label") %in% names(rate_df)))
  lbl_levels <- unname(CO2_RATE_OUTCOME_LABELS[CO2_RATE_OUTCOMES])
  rate_df$outcome_label <- factor(rate_df$outcome_label, levels = lbl_levels)

  ggplot2::ggplot(rate_df, ggplot2::aes(x = bin_mid, y = rate)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(color = "#2C7FB8", alpha = 0.8, size = 1.2) +
    ggplot2::geom_smooth(
      ggplot2::aes(weight = n_bin),
      method = "loess", span = 0.5, se = FALSE,
      color = "#D95F0E", linewidth = 0.8
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = vline_at, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ outcome_label, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = title,
      x = x_label,
      y = "Observed outcome rate",
      caption = paste0("Points are 1-mmHg bins; LOESS span = 0.5; dashed line at ", vline_at, " mmHg.")
    ) +
    theme_pub(base_size = 10)
}

# helper: derive log-scale odds-ratio axis limits from the observed confidence
# intervals so related figures stay comparable without manual tuning.
compute_or_axis_spec <- function(df_list, lo_col = "conf.low", hi_col = "conf.high",
                                 min_pow = -6, max_pow = 6,
                                 default_limits = c(0.25, 16)) {
  if (inherits(df_list, "data.frame")) df_list <- list(df_list)
  if (!is.list(df_list)) df_list <- list(df_list)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(df_list, function(df) {
    assert_is_df(df, context = "compute_or_axis_spec")
    stopifnot(all(c(lo_col, hi_col) %in% names(df)))
    lo <- df[[lo_col]]
    hi <- df[[hi_col]]
    c(lo, hi)
  }), use.names = FALSE)
  vals <- vals[is.finite(vals) & vals > 0]
  if (!length(vals)) {
    lo_pow <- floor(log2(default_limits[1]))
    hi_pow <- ceiling(log2(default_limits[2]))
  } else {
    lo_pow <- floor(log2(min(vals)))
    hi_pow <- ceiling(log2(max(vals)))
  }
  lo_pow <- max(lo_pow, min_pow)
  hi_pow <- min(hi_pow, max_pow)
  if (lo_pow > hi_pow) hi_pow <- lo_pow
  limits <- 2^c(lo_pow, hi_pow)
  breaks <- 2^(lo_pow:hi_pow)
  list(limits = limits, breaks = breaks)
}

# helper: apply a consistent odds-ratio y-axis specification to spline plots.
or_axis_scale <- function(spec) {
  list(
    ggplot2::scale_y_log10(
      breaks = spec$breaks,
      labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01)
    ),
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = spec$limits)
  )
}

# helper: derive probability-axis limits from the observed confidence bands so
# all probability panels share a stable visual range.
compute_prob_axis_spec <- function(df_list, lo_col = "p_LCL", hi_col = "p_UCL",
                                   floor = 0, ceiling = 1, pad = 0.02) {
  if (inherits(df_list, "data.frame")) df_list <- list(df_list)
  if (!is.list(df_list)) df_list <- list(df_list)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(df_list, function(df) {
    assert_is_df(df, context = "compute_prob_axis_spec")
    stopifnot(all(c(lo_col, hi_col) %in% names(df)))
    c(df[[lo_col]], df[[hi_col]])
  }), use.names = FALSE)
  vals <- vals[is.finite(vals)]
  if (!length(vals)) return(list(limits = c(floor, ceiling)))
  lo <- max(floor, min(vals) - pad)
  hi <- min(ceiling, max(vals) + pad)
  if (lo >= hi) {
    lo <- floor
    hi <- ceiling
  }
  list(limits = c(lo, hi))
}

# helper: apply a consistent probability y-axis specification to spline plots.
prob_axis_scale <- function(spec, accuracy = 1) {
  list(
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = accuracy)),
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = spec$limits)
  )
}

KEY_RESULTS_OUTCOME_LEVELS <- c("IMV", "NIV", "Death (60d)", "Hypercapnic RF")
KEY_RESULTS_OUTCOME_MAP <- c(
  imv_proc = "IMV",
  niv_proc = "NIV",
  death_60d = "Death (60d)",
  hypercap_resp_failure = "Hypercapnic RF",
  IMV = "IMV",
  NIV = "NIV",
  Death60d = "Death (60d)",
  HCRF = "Hypercapnic RF",
  "Death (60d)" = "Death (60d)",
  "Hypercapnic RF" = "Hypercapnic RF"
)
KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_LEVELS <- c("ABG", "VBG")
KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_COLORS <- c("ABG" = "#F8766D", "VBG" = "#00BFC4")
KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_LINETYPES <- c("ABG" = "solid", "VBG" = "longdash")
KEY_RESULTS_FIG_WIDTH <- 10.5
KEY_RESULTS_FIG_HEIGHT <- 11
MANUSCRIPT_FIG_BASE_SIZE <- 12
MANUSCRIPT_SPLINE_LINEWIDTH <- 0.95
MANUSCRIPT_CAT3_INTERVAL_LINEWIDTH <- 0.80
MANUSCRIPT_CAT3_POINT_SIZE <- 2.40
MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_DENSE_BASE_SIZE <- 10.5
MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_SPARSE_BASE_SIZE <- 11.5
MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_SEGMENT_LINEWIDTH <- 0.75
MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_POINT_SIZE <- c(`TRUE` = 3.30, `FALSE` = 2.35)
MANUSCRIPT_REFERENCE_LINEWIDTH <- 0.50
MANUSCRIPT_FIG_S1_WIDTH <- 9.5
MANUSCRIPT_FIG_S1_HEIGHT <- 7.25
MANUSCRIPT_FIG_S6_WIDTH <- 9.5
MANUSCRIPT_FIG_S6_HEIGHT <- 8.75
MANUSCRIPT_RIBBON_ALPHA <- 0.16
figure_style_update_audit <- tibble::tibble(
  component = c(
    "key_results_base_size",
    "spline_linewidth",
    "categorical_interval_linewidth",
    "categorical_point_size",
    "balance_dense_base_size",
    "balance_sparse_base_size",
    "balance_segment_linewidth",
    "balance_point_size_overall",
    "balance_point_size_regular",
    "reference_linewidth",
    "figure_s1_width",
    "figure_s1_height",
    "figure_s6_width",
    "figure_s6_height",
    "ribbon_alpha"
  ),
  value = c(
    MANUSCRIPT_FIG_BASE_SIZE,
    MANUSCRIPT_SPLINE_LINEWIDTH,
    MANUSCRIPT_CAT3_INTERVAL_LINEWIDTH,
    MANUSCRIPT_CAT3_POINT_SIZE,
    MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_DENSE_BASE_SIZE,
    MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_SPARSE_BASE_SIZE,
    MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_SEGMENT_LINEWIDTH,
    unname(MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_POINT_SIZE[["TRUE"]]),
    unname(MANUSCRIPT_BALANCE_POINT_SIZE[["FALSE"]]),
    MANUSCRIPT_REFERENCE_LINEWIDTH,
    MANUSCRIPT_FIG_S1_WIDTH,
    MANUSCRIPT_FIG_S1_HEIGHT,
    MANUSCRIPT_FIG_S6_WIDTH,
    MANUSCRIPT_FIG_S6_HEIGHT,
    MANUSCRIPT_RIBBON_ALPHA
  ),
  status = "configured",
  note = c(
    "Shared base size for Figure 2, Figure S4, and Figure S5 key-result helpers.",
    "Shared spline curve linewidth for odds-ratio and probability panels.",
    "Shared categorical forest interval linewidth.",
    "Shared categorical forest point size.",
    "Dense balance base size for Figure S1 with many covariate rows.",
    "Sparse balance base size for Figure S6 with fewer displayed rows.",
    "Shared Raw-to-IPW segment linewidth for love plots.",
    "Overall-row point size for balance diagnostics.",
    "Regular-row point size for balance diagnostics.",
    "Shared dashed reference-line linewidth.",
    "Figure S1 export width.",
    "Figure S1 export height.",
    "Figure S6 export width.",
    "Figure S6 export height.",
    "Shared confidence-ribbon alpha for spline panels."
  )
)
write_csv_safely(
  figure_style_update_audit,
  results_path("figure_style_update_audit.csv"),
  row_names = FALSE
)
KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_OUTCOME_LEVELS <- c("Hypercapnic RF", "NIV", "IMV", "Death (60d)")
KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_GROUP_COLORS <- c("ABG" = "#0072B2", "VBG" = "#D55E00")
KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_LINEWIDTH <- 1.35
KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_RIBBON_ALPHA <- 0.12

# helper: map raw outcome names from multiple upstream pipelines onto one
# ordered set of manuscript-facing labels for the spline key-results figures.
normalize_key_results_outcome <- function(x, context = "key-results") {
  out_chr <- as.character(x)
  mapped <- dplyr::recode(out_chr, !!!KEY_RESULTS_OUTCOME_MAP, .default = NA_character_)
  bad <- is.na(mapped)
  if (any(bad)) {
    stop(
      context, ": unmapped outcome labels: ",
      paste(sort(unique(out_chr[bad])), collapse = ", ")
    )
  }
  factor(mapped, levels = KEY_RESULTS_OUTCOME_LEVELS)
}

# helper: coerce cohort labels onto one stable ABG/VBG factor order.
normalize_key_results_group <- function(x, context = "key-results") {
  grp <- factor(as.character(x), levels = KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_LEVELS)
  if (any(is.na(grp))) {
    stop(
      context, ": unmapped group labels: ",
      paste(sort(unique(as.character(x)[is.na(grp)])), collapse = ", ")
    )
  }
  grp
}

# helper: keep paired ABG/VBG tables ordered by outcome first, then modality,
# so manuscript and supplement tables scan as IMV ABG/VBG, NIV ABG/VBG, etc.
arrange_key_results_outcome_group <- function(df, ...,
                                              outcome_col = "outcome",
                                              group_col = "group") {
  assert_is_df(df, context = "key-results row ordering")
  stopifnot(outcome_col %in% names(df), group_col %in% names(df))
  df |>
    dplyr::arrange(
      ...,
      factor(.data[[outcome_col]], levels = KEY_RESULTS_OUTCOME_LEVELS),
      factor(.data[[group_col]], levels = KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_LEVELS)
    )
}

# helper: standardize spline odds-ratio curve objects onto one common schema
# before they are combined into manuscript-facing figures.
normalize_key_results_or_df <- function(df, group = NULL, co2_col = "co2",
                                        outcome_col = "outcome",
                                        or_col = "OR", lcl_col = "LCL", ucl_col = "UCL",
                                        context = "key-results OR") {
  assert_is_df(df, context = context)
  stopifnot(outcome_col %in% names(df), or_col %in% names(df),
            lcl_col %in% names(df), ucl_col %in% names(df))
  if (!co2_col %in% names(df)) stop(context, ": missing CO2 column ", co2_col)
  group_vec <- if ("group" %in% names(df)) df$group else rep(group, nrow(df))
  if (is.null(group_vec)) stop(context, ": group must be supplied or present in data.")

  out <- tibble::tibble(
    group = normalize_key_results_group(group_vec, context = context),
    outcome = normalize_key_results_outcome(df[[outcome_col]], context = context),
    co2 = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[co2_col]])),
    OR = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[or_col]])),
    LCL = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[lcl_col]])),
    UCL = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[ucl_col]]))
  ) |>
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(co2), is.finite(OR), is.finite(LCL), is.finite(UCL),
                  OR > 0, LCL > 0, UCL > 0)

  if (!nrow(out)) stop(context, ": no finite OR rows after filtering.")
  out
}

# helper: standardize spline probability curves onto one common schema before
# the ABG/VBG overlays are assembled.
normalize_key_results_prob_df <- function(df, group = NULL, co2_col = "co2",
                                          outcome_col = "outcome",
                                          p_col = "p", lcl_col = "p_LCL", ucl_col = "p_UCL",
                                          context = "key-results probability") {
  assert_is_df(df, context = context)
  stopifnot(outcome_col %in% names(df), p_col %in% names(df),
            lcl_col %in% names(df), ucl_col %in% names(df))
  if (!co2_col %in% names(df)) stop(context, ": missing CO2 column ", co2_col)
  group_vec <- if ("group" %in% names(df)) df$group else rep(group, nrow(df))
  if (is.null(group_vec)) stop(context, ": group must be supplied or present in data.")

  out <- tibble::tibble(
    group = normalize_key_results_group(group_vec, context = context),
    outcome = normalize_key_results_outcome(df[[outcome_col]], context = context),
    co2 = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[co2_col]])),
    p = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[p_col]])),
    p_LCL = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[lcl_col]])),
    p_UCL = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df[[ucl_col]]))
  ) |>
    dplyr::filter(
      is.finite(co2), is.finite(p), is.finite(p_LCL), is.finite(p_UCL),
      dplyr::between(p, 0, 1), dplyr::between(p_LCL, 0, 1), dplyr::between(p_UCL, 0, 1)
    )

  if (!nrow(out)) stop(context, ": no finite probability rows after filtering.")
  out
}

# helper: stack one named list of odds-ratio curves into a single tidy frame
# keyed by outcome for the combined spline figure.
bind_key_results_or_curves <- function(curve_list, group, co2_col, context = "key-results OR") {
  stopifnot(is.list(curve_list), length(curve_list) > 0L, !is.null(names(curve_list)))
  dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(curve_list), function(outcome_nm) {
    curve_df <- curve_list[[outcome_nm]]
    curve_df$outcome <- outcome_nm
    normalize_key_results_or_df(
      curve_df,
      group = group,
      co2_col = co2_col,
      context = context
    )
  }))
}

# helper: stack one named list of predicted-probability curves into a single
# tidy frame keyed by outcome for the combined spline figure.
bind_key_results_prob_curves <- function(curve_list, group, co2_col,
                                         context = "key-results probability") {
  stopifnot(is.list(curve_list), length(curve_list) > 0L, !is.null(names(curve_list)))
  dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(curve_list), function(outcome_nm) {
    curve_df <- curve_list[[outcome_nm]]
    curve_df$outcome <- outcome_nm
    normalize_key_results_prob_df(
      curve_df,
      group = group,
      co2_col = co2_col,
      context = context
    )
  }))
}

# helper: build the odds-ratio half of the 4x2 spline key-results figure.
build_key_results_or_column <- function(or_df, column_title = "Odds ratio",
                                        x_label = "CO2 (mmHg)",
                                        show_row_labels = TRUE) {
  axis_spec <- compute_or_axis_spec(list(or_df), lo_col = "LCL", hi_col = "UCL")
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    or_df,
    ggplot2::aes(x = co2, y = OR, color = group, fill = group, linetype = group)
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_ribbon(
      ggplot2::aes(ymin = LCL, ymax = UCL),
      alpha = MANUSCRIPT_RIBBON_ALPHA,
      color = NA,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = MANUSCRIPT_SPLINE_LINEWIDTH) +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(
      yintercept = 1,
      linetype = "dashed",
      color = "grey25",
      linewidth = MANUSCRIPT_REFERENCE_LINEWIDTH
    ) +
    ggplot2::facet_grid(outcome ~ ., scales = "free_x", switch = "y") +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_COLORS) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_COLORS) +
    ggplot2::scale_linetype_manual(values = KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_LINETYPES) +
    or_axis_scale(axis_spec) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = column_title,
      x = x_label,
      y = "Odds ratio (log scale)",
      color = NULL,
      linetype = NULL
    ) +
    theme_pub(base_size = MANUSCRIPT_FIG_BASE_SIZE) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      legend.position = "top",
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      strip.placement = "outside",
      strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
      strip.text.y.left = if (show_row_labels) {
        ggplot2::element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold")
      } else {
        ggplot2::element_blank()
      },
      strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_blank()
    )
}

# helper: build the predicted-probability half of the 4x2 spline key-results
# figure using the same grouping and facet structure as the OR column.
build_key_results_prob_column <- function(prob_df, column_title = "Predicted probability",
                                          x_label = "CO2 (mmHg)",
                                          show_row_labels = FALSE) {
  axis_spec <- compute_prob_axis_spec(list(prob_df), lo_col = "p_LCL", hi_col = "p_UCL")
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    prob_df,
    ggplot2::aes(x = co2, y = p, color = group, fill = group, linetype = group)
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_ribbon(
      ggplot2::aes(ymin = p_LCL, ymax = p_UCL),
      alpha = MANUSCRIPT_RIBBON_ALPHA,
      color = NA,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = MANUSCRIPT_SPLINE_LINEWIDTH) +
    ggplot2::facet_grid(outcome ~ ., scales = "free_x", switch = "y") +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_COLORS) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_COLORS) +
    ggplot2::scale_linetype_manual(values = KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_LINETYPES) +
    prob_axis_scale(axis_spec) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = column_title,
      x = x_label,
      y = "Predicted probability",
      color = NULL,
      linetype = NULL
    ) +
    theme_pub(base_size = MANUSCRIPT_FIG_BASE_SIZE) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      legend.position = "top",
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      strip.placement = "outside",
      strip.background = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      strip.text.y.left = if (show_row_labels) {
        ggplot2::element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold")
      } else {
        ggplot2::element_blank()
      },
      strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_blank()
    )
}

# helper: assemble the full spline key-results figure with shared legend and
# caption once both columns have been built.
build_key_results_figure <- function(or_df, prob_df, figure_title, figure_caption) {
  or_plot <- build_key_results_or_column(or_df, show_row_labels = TRUE)
  prob_plot <- build_key_results_prob_column(prob_df, show_row_labels = FALSE)
  annotation_args <- list(title = figure_title)
  if (!is.null(figure_caption) && nzchar(figure_caption)) {
    annotation_args$caption <- figure_caption
  }
  (or_plot | prob_plot) +
    patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect", widths = c(1, 1)) +
    do.call(
      patchwork::plot_annotation,
      c(
        annotation_args,
        list(theme = ggplot2::theme(
          plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
          plot.caption = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0)
        ))
      )
    ) &
    ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "top")
}

# helper: build the poster-only odds-ratio version of the primary weighted
# spline figure without changing the manuscript 4x2 OR/probability figure.
audit_key_results_or_poster_axis <- function(plot_df) {
  assert_is_df(plot_df, context = "poster Figure 2 axis audit")
  stopifnot(all(c("outcome", "group", "co2", "OR", "LCL", "UCL") %in% names(plot_df)))
  central_df <- plot_df |>
    dplyr::group_by(.data$outcome, .data$group) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      co2_q05 = stats::quantile(.data$co2, probs = 0.05, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE),
      co2_q95 = stats::quantile(.data$co2, probs = 0.95, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE),
      central_range = .data$co2 >= .data$co2_q05 & .data$co2 <= .data$co2_q95
    ) |>
    dplyr::ungroup()
  central_hi <- suppressWarnings(max(central_df$UCL[central_df$central_range], na.rm = TRUE))
  central_lo <- suppressWarnings(min(central_df$LCL[central_df$central_range], na.rm = TRUE))
  if (!is.finite(central_hi)) central_hi <- 16
  if (!is.finite(central_lo)) central_lo <- 0.5
  y_upper <- dplyr::case_when(
    central_hi > 64 ~ 128,
    central_hi > 32 ~ 64,
    TRUE ~ 32
  )
  y_lower <- if (central_lo < 0.5) 0.25 else 0.5
  breaks <- 2^(floor(log2(y_lower)):ceiling(log2(y_upper)))
  breaks <- breaks[breaks >= y_lower & breaks <= y_upper]
  tibble::tibble(
    y_lower = y_lower,
    y_upper = y_upper,
    central_ucl_max = central_hi,
    central_lcl_min = central_lo,
    any_ci_truncated = any(plot_df$LCL < y_lower | plot_df$UCL > y_upper, na.rm = TRUE),
    central_ci_truncated = any(
      central_df$central_range & (central_df$LCL < y_lower | central_df$UCL > y_upper),
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    rationale = dplyr::if_else(
      y_upper > 32,
      paste0("Raised upper OR plotting limit to ", y_upper, " because the central modeled range required it."),
      "Kept conservative upper OR plotting limit; any truncation is confined to confidence-band tails."
    ),
    breaks = paste(breaks, collapse = "; ")
  )
}

build_key_results_or_poster_figure <- function(or_df) {
  plot_df <- normalize_key_results_or_df(
    or_df,
    context = "poster Figure 2 primary MI IPW OR"
  ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      outcome = factor(as.character(.data$outcome), levels = KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_OUTCOME_LEVELS)
    ) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$outcome))

  missing_outcomes <- setdiff(
    KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_OUTCOME_LEVELS,
    unique(as.character(plot_df$outcome))
  )
  if (length(missing_outcomes)) {
    stop(
      "Poster Figure 2 missing outcome(s): ",
      paste(missing_outcomes, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }
  missing_groups <- setdiff(KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_LEVELS, unique(as.character(plot_df$group)))
  if (length(missing_groups)) {
    stop(
      "Poster Figure 2 missing group(s): ",
      paste(missing_groups, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }

  axis_audit <- audit_key_results_or_poster_axis(plot_df)
  axis_spec <- list(
    limits = c(axis_audit$y_lower[[1]], axis_audit$y_upper[[1]]),
    breaks = as.numeric(strsplit(axis_audit$breaks[[1]], "; ", fixed = TRUE)[[1]])
  )
  x_range <- range(plot_df$co2, finite = TRUE)
  x_breaks <- pretty(x_range, n = 6)

  ggplot2::ggplot(
    plot_df,
    ggplot2::aes(x = co2, y = OR, color = group, fill = group, linetype = group)
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_ribbon(
      ggplot2::aes(ymin = LCL, ymax = UCL),
      alpha = KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_RIBBON_ALPHA,
      color = NA,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_LINEWIDTH) +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.65) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ outcome, ncol = 2) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_GROUP_COLORS) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = KEY_RESULTS_POSTER_GROUP_COLORS) +
    ggplot2::scale_linetype_manual(values = KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_LINETYPES) +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = x_breaks,
      limits = x_range,
      expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0))
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_log10(
      breaks = axis_spec$breaks,
      labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01)
    ) +
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = axis_spec$limits, clip = "off") +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = "Primary weighted spline odds ratios by blood gas type",
      subtitle = "Primary MI-logistic IPSW analysis; weighted curves target the eligible source cohort.",
      x = "pCO₂ (mmHg)",
      y = "Odds ratio, log scale",
      color = NULL,
      fill = NULL,
      linetype = NULL
    ) +
    theme_pub(base_size = 19, base_family = POSTER_FONT_FAMILY, legend_position = "top") +
    ggplot2::guides(
      fill = "none",
      color = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.8, alpha = 1)),
      linetype = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.8, alpha = 1))
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", hjust = 0),
      plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(size = 18, color = "grey20"),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.justification = "left",
      legend.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 19, face = "bold"),
      legend.key.width = grid::unit(1.1, "in"),
      axis.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 19),
      axis.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16),
      strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "grey92", color = NA),
      strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.major.y = ggplot2::element_line(color = "grey82", linewidth = 0.35),
      panel.spacing = grid::unit(1.0, "lines"),
      plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(12, 16, 12, 12)
    )
}

# helper: apply the shared odds-ratio axis specification to horizontal forest
# plots rather than vertical spline plots.
or_axis_scale_x <- function(spec) {
  list(
    ggplot2::scale_x_log10(
      breaks = spec$breaks,
      labels = scales::number_format(accuracy = 0.01)
    ),
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = spec$limits)
  )
}

# helper: apply the shared probability axis specification to horizontal forest
# plots rather than vertical spline plots.
prob_axis_scale_x <- function(spec, accuracy = 1) {
  list(
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = accuracy)),
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = spec$limits)
  )
}

CAT3_KEY_RESULTS_METHOD_LEVELS <- c(
  "Crude",
  "Adjusted",
  "Imputed",
  "Weighted and adjusted",
  "GBM-weighted"
)
CAT3_KEY_RESULTS_CONTRAST_LEVELS <- c("Low vs normal", "High vs normal")
CAT3_KEY_RESULTS_CATEGORY_LEVELS <- c("Low", "Normal", "High")
CAT3_KEY_RESULTS_GROUP_SHAPES <- c("ABG" = 16, "VBG" = 17)
CAT3_KEY_RESULTS_FIG_WIDTH <- 10.5
CAT3_KEY_RESULTS_FIG_HEIGHT <- 11
CAT3_FOREST_DODGE_OFFSET <- 0.16

# helper: coerce analysis-track labels onto one stable order for the
# categorical key-results tables and figures.
normalize_cat3_key_results_method <- function(x, context = "categorical key-results") {
  method <- factor(as.character(x), levels = CAT3_KEY_RESULTS_METHOD_LEVELS)
  if (any(is.na(method))) {
    stop(
      context, ": unmapped methods: ",
      paste(sort(unique(as.character(x)[is.na(method)])), collapse = ", ")
    )
  }
  method
}

# helper: coerce low/high contrasts onto one stable manuscript order.
normalize_cat3_key_results_contrast <- function(x, context = "categorical key-results") {
  contrast <- factor(as.character(x), levels = CAT3_KEY_RESULTS_CONTRAST_LEVELS)
